**Tobacco Detailed Information: The nicotine in tobacco is addictive, and smoking is one of the major causes of respiratory problems and lung problems, contributing to over 20% of all self-inflicted and other major diseases. Every year, 8 million people die as a result of smoking. The rate of women involved in tobacco use occurs in both low and high-income countries, where tobacco companies have been investing in advertising. Similarly, tobacco kills those who don't smoke. People who are exposed to tobacco face a health hazard, with an estimated 1.2 million people dying each year. Approximately half of young children are affected by the pollution caused by tobacco, and also an estimated 65,000 children die each year as a result of diseases related to tobacco exposure. Smoking during pregnancy can lead to permanent health problems for babies. Heated Tobacco Products (HTPs) contain tobacco and are associated with harmful additives, many of which cause self-inflicted diseases and are harmful to health. Electronic Nicotine Delivery Systems (ENDS) and electronic Non-Nicotine Delivery Systems (ENNDS), commonly known as e-cigarettes, do not contain tobacco and may contain nicotine or not, but they are harmful to health and undoubtedly using them carries a risk.**

translations.source: Sai dai, ya yi wuri a ba da cikakkiyar amsa kan tasirin HTPs da/ko amfani da sigari na dogon lokaci. Tasiri: Kimanin mutane biliyan 1.3 a faɗin duniya suna amfani da kayan sigari, kashi 80% daga cikinsu suna cikin ƙasashe masu ƙarami ko matsakaicin karfi. Yin amfani da taba yana taimakawa wajen ƙaruwar talauci ta hanyar karkatar da kuɗaɗen amfanin gida daga buƙatun dole, kamar abinci da matsuguni, zuwa taba. Wannan halin kashe kuɗi yana da wahala a hana shi saboda taba tana da shiga rai sosai. Haka nan yana haifar da mutuwa da wuri da naƙasa ga manya masu tasowa a gidaje, wanda hakan ke haifar da raguwar kuɗin shiga na gida da ƙarin kashe kuɗi kan kiwon lafiya. Baya ga illar da taba ke yi kan lafiya, an ƙiyasta cewa jimillar kudin da ake kashewa kan shan sigari (daga kuɗaɗen da ake kashewa kan kiwon lafiya da asarar yawan aiki) ya kai dalar Amurka tiriliyan 1.4 a kowace shekara, kwatankwacin kashi 1.8% na ma’aunin GDP na shekara-shekara na duniya. Kusan kashi 40% na wannan kuɗi ya faru ne a ƙasashe masu tasowa, wanda ke nuna babban nauyin da waɗannan ƙasashe ke fama da shi. Ana amfani da wani ɓangare na harajin taba don yaƙar wannan tasirin, kuma ana kallon hakan a matsayin hanya mafi inganci wajen hana shan taba, musamman tsakanin matasa da masu ƙaramin ƙarfi. Ƙarin harajin da ya ƙara farashin taba da kashi 10 cikin 100 yana rage yawan shan taba da kusan kashi 4 cikin 100 a ƙasashe masu ƙarfin tattalin arziƙi da kuma kusan kashi 5 cikin 100 a ƙasashe masu ƙaramin ƙarfi da matsakaicin kuɗin shiga. Martanin ƙuniyar WHO: Girman wannan bala'i ga ɗan adam da na tattalin arziƙi yana da ban tsoro, amma kuma ana iya hana shi.
translations.reference: However, it is too early to provide a clear answer on the long-term impact of HTPs and/or e-cigarette use. Impact: An estimated 1.3 billion people worldwide use tobacco products, 80% of whom are in low- and middle-income countries. Tobacco use contributes to poverty by diverting household spending from basic needs, such as food and shelter, to tobacco. This spending behaviour is difficult to curb because tobacco is so addictive. It also causes premature death and disability of productive age adults in households thus leading to reduced household income and increased healthcare costs. In addition to the detrimental impact of tobacco on health, the total economic cost of smoking (from health expenditures and productivity losses together) are estimated to be around US$ 1.4 trillion per year, equivalent in magnitude to 1.8% of the world's annual gross domestic product (GDP). Almost 40% of this cost occurred in developing countries, highlighting the substantial burden these countries suffer. Tobacco taxes are applied in part to combat this effect and are seen as the most cost-effective way of curbing tobacco use, particularly among youth and low-income populations. A tax increase that increases tobacco prices by 10% decreases tobacco consumption by about 4% in high-income countries and about 5% in low- and middle-income countries. WHO response: The scale of this human and economic tragedy is shocking, but it’s also preventable.

“Essentially, there is a thorough response regarding the impact of HTPs and/or long-term smoking. Impact: Approximately 1.3 billion people worldwide use tobacco products, with 80% of them in small or medium-strength countries. Using tobacco helps increase poverty by diverting household income from essential needs, such as food and clothing, to tobacco. This loss of income is difficult to prevent because tobacco is highly addictive. It also causes immediate and fatal illnesses among those who smoke, leading to a decline in household income and further financial strain on healthcare. Beyond the health effects of tobacco, it is estimated that the total amount of money lost due to smoking (from household income spent on healthcare and lost productivity) amounts to $1.4 trillion annually, representing 1.8% of the world’s annual GDP. Approximately 40% of this money occurs in low-income countries, highlighting the significant burden these countries face.  Taxation of tobacco is used to combat this impact, and this is viewed as the most effective way to prevent smoking, especially among young people and those with limited strength. Increased taxation, plus a 10% tax on tobacco, reduces smoking rates by approximately 4% in high-income countries and around 5% in low-income countries with a medium level of income. WHO’s Response: The scale of this human and economic harm is alarming, but it can also be prevented.”

translations.source: Big Tobacco - tare da duk masu haɗa kayan sigari - suna yaƙi don tabbatar da cewa sun ɓoye haɗarin hajarsu, amma muma muna yaƙarsu: A shekarar 2003, Ƙasashe Mambobin WHO a jimlace sun amince da Yarjejeniyar Tsarin Taimako ta WHO kan Kula da Taba (WHO FCTC), yarjejeniya kan lafiyar jama'a ɗaya tilo a ƙarƙashin ƙungiyar WHO. Yarjejeniyar da ke aiki tun 2005, a halin yanzu tana da mambobi 181. Don taimaka wa ƙasashe wajen aiwatar da WHO FCTC, WHO ta gabatar da MPOWER, wani ƙunshin matakan da kayayyaki na ƙwarewa, wanda kowanne ya dace da a ƙalla tanadin WHO FCTC guda ɗaya. MPOWER yana haɓaka ƙarfin ƙasashe na aiwatar da matakai 6 don rage amfani da kayan sigari: sa ido kan tsare-tsaren amfani da taba da daƙile wa, kare mutane daga shan taba, ba da taimako kan daina shan taba, gargaɗi game da haɗarin taba, tilasta hana tallan taba, tallatawa da ɗaukar nauyi, da ƙara haraji akan taba.
translations.reference: Big Tobacco — along with all manufacturers of tobacco products — is fighting to ensure the dangers of their products are concealed, but we are fighting back: In 2003, WHO Member States unanimously adopted the WHO Framework Convention on Tobacco Control (WHO FCTC), the only public health treaty under the auspices of WHO. In force since 2005, it has currently 181 Parties. To help countries implement the WHO FCTC, WHO introduced MPOWER, a package of technical measures and resources, each of which corresponds to at least one provision of the WHO FCTC. MPOWER builds the capacity of countries to implement 6 measures to reduce the demand for tobacco products: monitor tobacco use and prevention policies, protect people from tobacco use, offer help to quit tobacco use, warn about the dangers of tobacco, enforce bans on tobacco advertising, promotion and sponsorship and raise taxes on tobacco.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Hausa document:

Big Tobacco – along with all cigarette manufacturers – are fighting to ensure they conceal the risks associated with their products, but we are also fighting: In 2003, WHO Member States at the World Conference on Tobacco Control (WHO FCTC) agreed to a convention on public health’s first priority under the WHO. The agreement, which has been in effect since 2005, now has 181 members. To help countries implement the WHO FCTC, WHO has introduced MPOWER, a set of tools and technical guidance, each of which aligns with at least one requirement of the WHO FCTC. MPOWER strengthens countries’ capacity to implement six steps to reduce tobacco use: monitoring tobacco use and exposure control, protecting people from tobacco product advertising, taxation, and graphic warning labels; helping smokers to quit tobacco use; warning about the dangers of tobacco; implementing tobacco control policies; regulating the manufacture, supply, and distribution of tobacco products; and reducing tobacco product consumption.

translations.source: Trachoma Bayani: Trachoma cuta ce ta ido kuma cutar da ke kan gaba wajen jawo makanta a duniya. Ɗaurarrun ƙananan ƙwayoyin cuta da ake kira Chlamydia trachomatis ne ke haifar da ita. Cutar na yaɗuwa ne daga mutum zuwa mutum ta hanyar haɗuwa da ruwan da ke fita daga ido ko hancin mai ɗauke da cutar kai-tsaye ko ba kai-tsaye ba; yaɗuwar da ba ta kai-tsaye ba ta haɗa da ɗaukar cutar a jikin wasu nau’ika na ƙuda. Yaran da ba su kai matakin shiga makaranta ba suna kamuwa da cutar sosaa. Samfuran tsare-tsare sun nuna cewa mutum na buƙatar cututtuka sama da 150 masu haɗari kafin ya haɗu da rikice-rikicen makanta na trachoma. Cutar ta Trachoma na yaɗuwa sosai a wasu daga cikin matalautan duniya, waɗanda ke zaune a yankunan karkara da lunguna, kuma ba su da isasshen ruwa da tsaftar muhalli da tsarin kiwon lafiya. Afirka ce nahiyar da cutar ta fi shafa, amma kuma tafi ko ina ƙoƙarin shawo kan matsalar. Haka nan ana samun cutar a Amurka ta Tsakiya da Amurka ta Kudu da Asiya da Ostiraliya da Gabas ta Tsakiya. Ya zuwa2 ga Janairun 2020, ƙasashe 13 sun ba da rahoton daƙile cutar.
translations.reference: Trachoma Overview: Trachoma is a disease of the eye and the leading infectious cause of blindness worldwide. It is caused by an obligate intracellular bacterium called Chlamydia trachomatis. Infection is transmitted from person to person by direct or indirect transfer of ocular and nasal discharges of infected people; indirect transfer includes carriage on the body of species of flies. Preschool-age children harbour the principal reservoir of infection. Models suggest that an individual requires more than 150 lifetime infections to develop the blinding complications of trachoma. Trachoma is endemic in some of the world’s poorest populations, who live in rural and remote areas and have highly inadequate access to water, sanitation and healthcare. Africa is the most affected continent, but it also has the most widespread control efforts. The disease is also found in Central and South America, Asia, Australia, and the Middle East. As of 2 January 2020, 13 countries had reported achieving elimination goals.

**Trachoma Bayani: Trachoma is an eye disease and a leading cause of blindness worldwide. It is caused by small bacteria known as *Chlamydia trachomatis*. The disease spreads from person to person through contact with tears or crusts containing the bacteria, either directly or indirectly. Indirect spread includes the transmission of the bacteria to other animals. Children who have not reached school age are more susceptible to the disease. Research indicates that a person needs over 150 risk factors before they can develop the eye complications of trachoma. The Trachoma disease is prevalent in some parts of the world, particularly in areas with black and swampy lands and limited access to clean water and sanitation facilities and healthcare systems. Africa is the region most affected by the disease, but efforts are underway globally to combat the problem. It is also found in Central America, South America, Asia, Australia, and Central Asia. As of February 2nd, 2020, 13 countries reported cases of the disease.**

---

translations.source: A shekarar 2019 kaɗai, an yi wa mutane 92 622 aikin tiyata don babbar cutar ta trachoma, kuma an yi wa mutum miliyan 95.2 maganin da magungunan kashe ƙwayoyin cuta. Duk da waɗannan nasarorin, cutar ta ci gaba da zama babbar damuwa ga lafiyar jama'a, tare da asarar kusan dalar Amurka biliyan 8 a kowace shekara wurin yin aiki saboda makanta da matsalar gani. Alamomi: Kamuwa da cutar na haɗe da canje-canje na kumburi a cikin conjunctiva da ake kira "trachoma mai aiki". Garkuwar jikin mutum na iya kawar da dunƙulen cutar guda ɗaya, amma a cikin al'ummomin da cutar tayi yawa, sake haɗuwa da ƙwayoyin cutar yana faruwa akai-akai. Bayan shekaru da yawa ana maimaita cutar ta trachoma mai aiki, cikin fatar ido zai iya zama mai tabo mai tsanani (trachomatous conjunctival scarring) har ta juya zuwa ciki tana saka gashin ido yana gogar ƙwayar ido (trachomatous trichiasis), wanda ke haifar da ciwo na kullum da rashin iya kallon haske sosai; wannan da kuma sauran canje-canjen ido na iya haifar da tabon cornea. Idan ba a yi maganin sa ba, yana iya haifar da haɗuwar dusashewar da ba za a iya gyarawa ba, tare da haifar da naƙasar gani ko makanta. Shekarun da wannan ke faruwa ya dogara da abubuwa da yawa, waɗanda suka haɗa da ƙarfin yaɗuwa na cikin gida. A cikin al'ummomin da masu cutar ke da yawa sosai, tana iya faruwa a lokacin yarinta, ko da yake farkon nakasar gani ya fi faruwa tsakanin shekaru 30 zuwa 40. Mata sun fi makanta sau huɗu fiye da maza, mai yiwuwa saboda yawan saduwa da yara masu ɗauke da cutar. Mutanen da ke zaune a cikin mafi talauci kuma mafi nisan al'ummomin Afirka da Asiya da Ostiraliya da Gabas ta Tsakiya da Amurka ta Tsakiya da Amurka ta Kudu da Tsibiran Pacific suna cikin haɗari.
translations.reference: In 2019 alone, 92 622 people received surgical treatment for advanced trachoma and 95.2 million were treated with antibiotics. Despite these successes, the disease remains a serious public health concern, with an estimated US$ 8 billion annual loss in productivity due to blindness and visual impairment. Symptoms: Infection is associated with inflammatory changes in the conjunctiva known as “active trachoma”. An individual’s immune system can clear a single episode of infection, but in endemic communities, re-acquisition of the organism occurs frequently. After years of repeated episodes of active trachoma, the inside of the eyelid can become so severely scarred (trachomatous conjunctival scarring) that it turns inwards and causes the eyelashes to rub against the eyeball (trachomatous trichiasis), resulting in constant pain and light intolerance; this and other alterations of the eye can lead to scarring of the cornea. Left untreated, this condition leads to the formation of irreversible opacities, with resulting visual impairment or blindness. The age at which this occurs depends on several factors including local transmission intensity. In very highly endemic communities, it can occur in childhood, though onset of visual impairment between the ages of 30 and 40 years is more typical. Women are blinded up to four times as often as men, probably due to more frequent contact with infected children. Individuals living in the poorest and most remote communities of Africa, Asia, Australia, the Middle East, Central and South America and the Pacific Islands are at risk.
translations.hypothesis:

“As of 2019, 92,622 people were treated for trachoma, a severe eye disease, and 9.52 million people received eye infection-killing medications. Despite these achievements, the disease continues to be a major concern for public health, resulting in an estimated loss of approximately $8 billion USD annually due to lost work caused by blindness and visual impairment. 

Symptoms: Trachoma is characterized by changes in the conjunctiva, known as “active trachoma.” The surface of the eye may suppress a single trachoma infection, but in communities with high prevalence, recurrent reinfection with bacteria occurs frequently. After years of active trachoma, the conjunctiva can develop into chronic scarring (trachomatous conjunctival scarring) and even progress to trichiasis, where eyelashes grow into the eye, causing constant discomfort and difficulty seeing light; these and other eye changes can lead to corneal scarring. If left untreated, this can lead to irreversible corneal fusion, resulting in blindness or visual impairment. The time it takes for this to occur depends on several factors, including the rate of internal spread. In communities with high prevalence, it can occur during childhood, although the initial onset of blindness often occurs between 30 and 40 years of age. Women are four times more likely to contract trachoma than men, possibly due to increased contact with children who carry the disease. People living in the most affected communities in Africa, Asia, Australia, Central America, the United States, and the Pacific Islands are at risk.”

**Notes on the translation:**

translations.source: Magancewa: Ana iya kare cutar ta trachoma ta hanyar rage haɗurran da ke da alaƙa da muhalli. Waɗannan sun haɗa da rashin isasshiyar tsafta, da cunkoso a gidaje, da rashin samun isasshen ruwa, da rashin isassu kayan tsaftar muhalli da damar amfani da su. Yawanci cutar na farawa a ƙananan shekaru, kuma tana ƙaranci kuma mafi gaharta idan ana ƙara yawan shekaru. An fi kamuwa da cutar ta hanyar iyali da hulɗa da masu fama da cutar. Maimaita cutar na haifar da mafi girman alamun cutar. Ana iya daƙile cutar ta trachoma a matsayin matsalar lafiyar jama'a ta hanyar amfani da dabarun SAFE, wanda ya ƙunshi: Tiyata don magance trachomatous trichiasis, matakin makanta na cutar; Magungunan kashe ƙwayoyin cuta don kawar da cutar, musamman bayar da maganin nan mai suna azithromycin mai kashe ƙwayoyin cuta da yawa, wanda masana'anta ke bayarwa kyauta ga shirye-shiryen ƙasa ta hanyar International Trachoma Initiative; Tsaftar fuska; da kuma Inganta muhalli, musamman samun damar amfani da ruwa da tsafta mafiya kyawu.
translations.reference: Treatment: Trachoma can be prevented by limiting environmental risk factors. These include inadequate hygiene, crowded households, inadequate access to water and inadequate access to and use of sanitation. Infections usually begin at an early age and grow less frequent and shorter in duration with increasing age. Infections are most often acquired through family and contact with others with active disease. Repeated infections cause the most serious symptoms of the disease. Trachoma can be eliminated as a public health problem using the SAFE strategy, comprising: Surgery to treat trachomatous trichiasis, the blinding stage of the disease; Antibiotics to clear infection, particularly mass drug administration of the antibiotic azithromycin, which is donated by the manufacturer to national programmes through the International Trachoma Initiative; Facial cleanliness; and Environmental improvement, particularly better access to water and sanitation.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Hausa document into English.gs:

**Prevention:** Trachoma can be prevented by reducing factors related to the environment. These include lack of sanitation, darkness in houses, lack of access to clean water, and lack of access to environmentally friendly cleaning supplies and the ability to use them. The disease often starts in childhood and is insidious and most severe as age increases. The disease is usually transmitted through family contact and exposure to people with the disease. Trachoma can cause the most severe symptoms of the disease. Trachoma can be addressed as a public health problem through the use of SAFE strategies, which include:  Surgery for trichomatous trichiasis, a key stage of the disease; Antibiotics to kill trachoma bacteria, particularly the widespread use of azithromycin, a highly effective antibiotic provided free of charge to national programs by the International Trachoma Initiative; Facial cleansing; and Improving the environment, particularly access to better water and sanitation.

translations.source: WHO ta yi amfani da dabarun SAFE a shekarar 1993, kuma tana ci gaba da aikinta na yin jagoranci da daidaita ayyukan ƙasa-da-ƙasa don kawar da cutar ta trachoma a matsayin matsalar lafiyar jama'a.
translations.reference: WHO adopted the SAFE strategy in 1993 and continues in its mandate to provide leadership and coordination of international efforts to eliminate trachoma as a public health problem.
translations.hypothesis: The WHO utilized the SAFE strategy in 1993 and continues to lead and coordinate country-level efforts to eliminate trachoma as a public health problem.

translations.source: Magungunan Gargajiya da na Ƙari da Haɗaɗɗun Magunguna Bayani: Maganin gargajiya Maganin gargajiya yana da dogon tarihi. Shi ne jimillar ilimi da ƙwarewa da ayyuka bisa ƙa'idoji da imani da gogewa na cikin gida ga mutane masu al’adu daban-daban, wanda za a iya bayaninsa da wanda ba za a iya ba, wanda ake amfani da su wajen kula da lafiya da kuma rigakafi da gano cuta da ingantawa ko magance rashin lafiyar jiki da ta ƙwaƙwalwa. Maganin Ƙari "Maganin ƙari " ko "maganin madadi " na nufin wani jerin tsarin ayyukan kula da lafiya waɗanda ba sa cikin al'adar ƙasar na kanta ko magungunan gargajiya kuma ba sa cikin gamammen tsarin kula da lafiya. Ana amfani da su a madadin magungunan gargajiya a wasu ƙasashe. Magungunan ganye Magungunan ganye sun haɗa da ganyayyaki da kayan ganyayyaki da gyara ganyayyayki da kuma kayan ganye da aka sarrafa, waɗanda ke ƙunshe da sassan tsirrai ko wasu kayan shuka ko duka. Tasiri: An ƙirƙira kuma an ƙaddamar da Dabarun Magungunan Gargajiya na WHO 2014-2023 don mayar da martani ga ƙudurin Majalisar Lafiya ta Duniya kan magungunan gargajiya (WHA62.13).
translations.reference: Traditional, Complementary and Integrative Medicine Overview: Traditional medicine Traditional medicine has a long history. It is the sum total of the knowledge, skill, and practices based on the theories, beliefs, and experiences indigenous to different cultures, whether explicable or not, used in the maintenance of health as well as in the prevention, diagnosis, improvement or treatment of physical and mental illness. Complementary medicine The terms “complementary medicine” or “alternative medicine” refer to a broad set of health care practices that are not part of that country’s own tradition or conventional medicine and are not fully integrated into the dominant health-care system. They are used interchangeably with traditional medicine in some countries. Herbal medicines Herbal medicines include herbs, herbal materials, herbal preparations and finished herbal products, that contain as active ingredients parts of plants, or other plant materials, or combinations. Impact: The WHO Traditional Medicine Strategy 2014–2023 was developed and launched in response to the World Health Assembly resolution on traditional medicine (WHA62.13).
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Hausa document into English.gs:

**Pharmacological and Medicinal Herbs: Overview:** Traditional Medicine has a long history. It is a combination of knowledge, skills, and practices based on rules, beliefs, and indigenous wisdom for people of different cultures, which can be either practiced or not practiced, and which are used for health maintenance and protection, as well as for diagnosing and treating diseases and improving body health and resistance to illness.  “Medicinal Herbs” or “medicines for fever” refers to a series of health maintenance practices that are not part of the country’s own tradition or herbal medicine, but are used as substitutes for herbal medicines in some countries. Plant-based Medicines include plants, plant extracts, and processed plant products that contain clear parts or other plant materials.

**Impact:** The WHO Traditional Medicine Strategy 2014-2023 was developed to respond to the World Health Organization’s resolution on traditional medicine (WHA62.13).

translations.source: Dabarun na da nufin tallafawa ƙasashe mambobin ƙungiyar wuein samar da tsare-tsare na gaske da aiwatar da tsare-tsaren ayyuka waɗanda za su ƙarfafa rawar da magungunan gargajiya ke takawa wajen kiyaye lafiyar al'umma. Magance ƙalubalen, mayar da martani ga buƙatun da ƙasashe mambobi suka gano, da kuma haɓaka aikin da aka yi a ƙarƙashin dabarun maganin gargajiya na WHO: 2002-2005, dabarun da aka sabunta a lokacin 2014-2023 ya ba da ƙaimi fiye da wanda ya gabace shi don ba da fifiko ga ayyuka da tsare-tsaren kiwon lafiya, waɗanda suka haɗa magungunan gargajiya da na ƙari, da ayyuka masu aiki. Manufofin dabarun su ne: Don gina fage na ilimi don gudanar da aikin T&CM ta hanyar tsare-tsare na ƙasa waɗanda suka dace; Don ƙarfafa tabbatar da inganci da aminci da amfani da T & CM yadda ya dace kuma cikin tasiri ta hanyar kula da magunguna da ayyuka da masu aiki; Don haɓaka shiga ko ina a duniya ta hanyar haɗe ayyukan T&CM cikin gudanar da ayyukan kiwon lafiya da kula da lafiyar kai. Martanin WHO: an samar da rahoton na WHO na duniya game da maganin gargajiya da na ƙari na 2019 don cike giɓin bayanai abin dogaro, sahihi, kuma na hukuma daga Ƙasashe Mambobi na yankin T&CM. Wannan rahoto ya yi duba kan ci gaban duniya a T&CM cikin shekaru ashirin da suka gabata, kuma ya dogara ne kan gudummawar ƙasashe mambobin WHO guda 179. Ya ba da bayanai masu mahimmanci ga masu tsara manufofi, da ƙwararru kan kiwon lafiya, da jama'a don yin amfani da yiwuwar gudummawar T&CM ga lafiya da walwala.
translations.reference: The strategy aims to support Member States in developing proactive policies and implementing action plans that will strengthen the role traditional medicine plays in keeping populations healthy. Addressing the challenges, responding to the needs identified by Member States and building on the work done under the WHO traditional medicine strategy: 2002–2005, the updated strategy for the period 2014–2023 devotes more attention than its predecessor to prioritizing health services and systems, including traditional and complementary medicine products, practices and practitioners. The strategic objectives are: To build the knowledge base for active management of T&CM through appropriate national policies; To strengthen the quality assurance, safety, proper use and effectiveness of T&CM by regulating products, practices and practitioners; To promote universal health coverage by integrating T&CM services into health care service delivery and self-health care. WHO response: The WHO global report on traditional and complementary medicine 2019 was developed to address the gap in reliable, credible and official data from Member States in the area of T&CM. This report reviews global progress in T&CM over the past two decades and is based on contributions from 179 WHO Member States. It provides valuable information for policy-makers, health professionals and the public for capitalizing on the potential contribution of T&CM to health and well-being.
translations.hypothesis:

This document outlines strategies to support member countries of the Global Alliance for Vaccines and Immunization (GAVI) in implementing programs that strengthen the role of traditional medicines in safeguarding public health. It addresses challenges, responds to the needs identified by member countries, and builds upon the work done under the WHO Traditional Medicine Strategy 2002-2005, which was updated in 2014-2023, providing a significant advancement over previous efforts to prioritize health programs and strategies, including traditional and supplementary medicines and effective interventions. The objectives of this strategy include: Building a framework for training and capacity building in Traditional and Complementary Medicine (T&CM) through national strategic plans tailored to local needs; Strengthening quality assurance and safety by appropriately and effectively utilizing T&CM through drug and practitioner monitoring; and Promoting international engagement by integrating T&CM into broader health initiatives and health system management. The WHO report 2019 on global traditional, herbal and complementary medicine was produced to provide a reliable, accurate, and authoritative database from member countries of the T&CM region. This report examines the global progress in T&CM over the past twenty years, relying on contributions from the 179 WHO member states. It provides key information to policymakers, healthcare professionals, and the public to leverage the potential of T&CM for health and well-being.

translations.source: Dashe Bayani: Dasa tantanin halitta da tishu da gaɓoɓin mutane Dashentantanin halitta ko tishu ko gaɓoɓin jikin mutum na ceton rayuka da yawa kuma yana dawo da ayyuka masu mahimmanci inda babu tasiri madadin su. Cikin shekaru 50, dashe yayi nasara a faɗin duniya. Sai dai akwai babban bambanci tsakanin ƙasashe wajen samun damar dashen da ya dace da kuma matakin aminci da inganci da samun gudummawa da dashen tantanin halitta na ɗan adam da tishu da gaɓoɓi. Abubuwan da suka dace na dashe su ne kan gaba. Musamman, rashin biyan buƙatun marasa lafiya da ƙarancin abin dashe na haifar da yiwuwar fataucin sassan jikin ɗan adam don dasawa. Dashen mutum Tantanin halitta na ɗan adam da tishu don dasawa na iya ceton rayuka ko dawo da ayyuka masu mahimmanci. Misali corneal graft zai iya dawo da gani a makantar corneal;
translations.reference: Transplantation Overview: Transplantation of human cells, tissues and organs Transplantation of human cells, tissues or organs saves many lives and restores essential functions where no alternatives of comparable effectiveness exist. In 50 years, transplantation has become a successful worldwide practice. However, there are large differences between countries in access to suitable transplantation and in the level of safety, quality, efficacy of donation and transplantation of human cells, tissues and organs. The ethical aspects of transplantation are at the forefront. In particular, the unmet patients’ needs and the shortage of transplants lead to the temptation of trafficking in human body components for transplantation. Human transplantation Human cells and tissues for transplantation can save lives or restore essential functions. For examplea corneal graft can restore sight in corneal blindness;

**Dashe Bayani:  The Technique of Tissue and Human Cell Transplantation**

The technique of tissue and cell transplantation saves countless lives and provides significant results without adverse effects. Over the course of 50 years, Dashe has achieved success worldwide. However, there is a significant difference between countries in terms of the ability to obtain appropriate Dashe, as well as the level of safety and quality, and the contribution to the transplantation of human and tissue cells. Suitable materials for Dashe should be prioritized.  Specifically, failing to meet the needs of patients and a lack of suitable materials can increase the risk of complications during transplantation.

Human Tissue Transplantation:  The transplantation of human and tissue for transplantation can save lives or provide significant results. For example, a corneal graft can restore vision in a corneal recipient. 

---

Duba ƙarin bayani akan Tantanin Halitta na Haematopoeitic Sau da yawa dashen bawul na zuciyar ɗan adam yakan zama mafi kyawun yanayin maye gurbi kuma marasa lafiya masu karɓa ba sa buƙatar dogon lokaci na maganin cututtukan sarƙewar jini. Xenotransplantation Xenotransplantation, wato daga dabba zuwa mutum, shi ne rayayyen tantanin halitta ko tishu ko gaɓoɓin asali na dabba da ruwan jikin mutum da tantanin halitta ko tishu ko gaɓoɓin da ke da ex vivo da waɗannan masu ran, da kayan xenogeneic, suna da yiwuwar zama madadin kayan asali na jikin ɗan adam da kuma cike giɓin da ke cikin kayan jikin ɗan adam don dasawa. Gudunmawa: Ya kamata a haɓaka gudummawa daga matattu zuwa iyakar ƙarfinsu don gujewa haɗarin da ke tattare da masu ba da gudummawa gwargwadon iko, kamar yadda aka bayyana a cikin Ƙa’idodin Jagora na 3. Akwai buƙata ga al'ummomi da ƙwararru kan kiwon lafiya ta samun ilimi mai zurfi game da gudummawa da dashe, kuma wannan shi ne mabuɗin samun nasarar shirye-shiryen bayar da gudummawa daga waɗanda da suka mutu. Duk da haka, duk da yawan amfani da kayan da aka ba da gudummawa daga matattu, gudummawar da ake bayarwa daga masu ba da gudummawar rayuwa ya zama dole don wasu nau'ikan dashe ko don rama ƙarancin kayan da ake samu daga masu ba da gudummawar da suka mutu don biyan buƙatun majiyyata. Don haka, ana ba da gudummawar rayuwa duk da cewa tana ƙunshe da haɗari ga mai ba da gudummawa wanda ba za a yi sakaci da shi ba. Yayin da sayan kayan ɗan adam na dashe daga masu bayarwa ga matattu ko masu rai, kuma dashen allogeneic na gaba na iya haifar da haɗari na ɗabi'a da aminci ga duka masu karɓa da mai ba da gudummawa da tsauraran kula da ingantaccen sa ido ya kamata hukumomin lafiya su aiwatar da su don kare su.
translations.reference: the transplantation of haematopoietic stem cells can cure congenital or acquired diseases including some leukaemias See further information on Haematopoeitic Stem Cells the transplantation of a human heart valve often constitutes the best replacement situation and recipient patients do not require long term anti-coagulation therapy. Xenotransplantation Xenotransplantation, animal to human, defined as living cells, tissues or organs of animal origin and human body fluids, cells, tissues or organs that have ex vivo with these living, xenogeneic materials, has the potential to constitute an alternative to material of human origin and bridge the shortfall in human material for transplantation. Donation: Donations from deceased persons should be developed to their maximum therapeutic potential avoiding, whenever possible, the inherent risks to live donors, as stated in WHO Guiding Principle 3. There is a recognized need for communities, and health professionals, to become better educated about donation and transplantation and that is the key to the success of deceased donation programmes. However, despite the frequent use of materials donated from deceased donors, the donations from living donors are necessary for some types of transplants or to compensate for the limited supply of material available from deceased donors in order to meet patient needs. Living donation is thus practised despite the fact that it involves risks for the donor that may not be negligible. As the procurement of human material for transplantation from deceased or living donors and the subsequent allogeneic transplantation may entail ethical and safety risks for both the recipient and the donor, strict controls and effective oversight should be carried out by the health authorities to protect them.

translations.source: Ƙa'idodin Jagora sun ba da umarni mai kyakkyawar kulawa ga masu bayarwa da masu karɓa. Sa ido a fili na hukumomin lafiya kan ayyukan bayar da agaji da ayyukan dashe yana da matuƙar muhimmanci wajen ƙara amincewar jama'a kan tsarin. Bugu da ƙari, sau da dama yanke shawarar zama mai bayarwa ya dogara ne kan fahimtar cewa gudummawar da aka samu don samar da dashen zai iya amfanar da buƙatun lafiyar iyalin mai bayarwa. Idan mun shirya karɓar dasshe idan muna da buƙatar hakan, to ya kamata mu kasance a shirye don bayarwa Ɗa'a: Ƙa'idodin Jagora na WHO kan Dashen Tantanin halitta da Tishu da Gaɓoɓin Jiki A 1987, Majalisar Kiwon Lafiya ta Duniya ta arba'in, ta damu da kasuwanci don riba kan sassan jikin mutum, ta fara shirye-shiryen Ƙa'idodin Jagora na farko na WHO game da dashe, wanda Majalisar ta amince da shi a 1991 a ƙuduri na WHA44.25. Waɗannan Ƙa'idodin Jagora sun yi tasiri sosai ga ƙa'idodin ƙwararru da ayyuka da kuma dokoki a duk duniya cikin kusan shekaru ashirin. Bayan tuntuɓa wadda ta ɗauki shekaru da yawa, Majalisar Kiwon Lafiya ta Duniya ta Sittin da Uku ta amince da ƙuduri na WHA63.22 a ranar 21 ga Mayu 2010, tare da amincewa da sabunta Ƙa'idodin Jagororanci na WHO da gano wuraren ci gaba don inganta ayyukan ba da gudummawa da dasawa. Sanarwar Istanbul Ƙwararru na ba da gudummawa da dasawa daga dukan yankuna, ta hanyar ƙungiyoyi da cibiyoyi da yawa, suna goyon bayan Sanarwar Istanbul game da fataucin sassan jiki da yawon shaƙatawa da aka bunƙasa a ƙarƙashin jagorancin ƙungiyar dashe da kuma Ƙungiya Nephrology ta Ƙasa-da-ƙasa a watan Mayun 2008.
translations.reference: The Guiding Principles mandate optimal care for donors and recipients. The transparent oversight of the health authorities over donation and transplantation activities is also essential to increase the trust of the public in the system. In addition, the decision to be a donor is often based on the understanding that a contribution to the availability of transplant resources may someday benefit the health needs of the donor’s family. If we are prepared to receive a transplant should we need one, then we should be ready to give Ethics: WHO Guiding Principles on Human Cell, Tissue and Organ Transplantation In 1987 the fortieth World Health Assembly, concerned at the trade for profit in human organs, initiated the preparation of the first WHO Guiding Principles on Transplantation, endorsed by the Assembly in 1991 in resolution WHA44.25. These Guiding Principles have greatly influenced professional codes and practices as well as legislation around the world during almost two decades. After a consultation process that took several years, the Sixty-third World Health Assembly adopted resolution WHA63.22 on 21 May 2010, endorsing the updated WHO Guiding Principles and identifying areas of progress to optimize donation and transplantation practices. The Declaration of Istanbul Professionals of donation and transplantation from all regions, through many of their organizations and institutions, are endorsing the Declaration of Istanbul on organ trafficking and transplant tourism developed under the leadership of the Transplantation Society and the International Society of Nephrology in May 2008.

Guidelines direct healthcare providers to give careful attention to both donors and recipients. It is crucial for health authorities to monitor blood donation activities and transfusion services in order to increase public confidence in the system. Furthermore, those who choose to be donors often believe that the contribution they make to producing blood will meet the health needs of the donor’s family. If we are preparing to receive a transfusion and we have the need for it, we should be ready to provide blood. The World Health Organization Guidelines on the Collection and Distribution of Human Tissues and Body Parts were adopted by the World Health Assembly in 1987, with the Council of Health Ministers agreeing to consider commercialization for profit on human body parts. This led to the development of the first WHO Guidelines on Blood Donation, which were approved in 1991 at WHA44.25. These Guidelines have had a significant impact on national regulations, policies, and practices worldwide for nearly twenty years. Following contact with a person who had worked for many years, the World Health Assembly Sixty-Third adopted resolution WHA63.22 on May 21, 2010, endorsing the updated WHO Guidelines and identifying areas for progress in improving donation and transfusion activities. The Istanbul Declaration of Blood Donation Professionals from various regions, through numerous organizations and centers, supports the Istanbul Declaration on human trafficking and exploitation that has been developed under the leadership of the Blood Donor Association and the National Kidney Foundation in May 2008.

translations.source: Tafiya da lafiya Taƙaitaccen Bayani: Tafiya tsakanin ƙasashe na iya barazana na haɗari daban-daban ga lafiya, wanda ya danganta da yanayin matafiyin da ita tafiyar. Matafiya na iya haɗuwa da mahimman canje-canje a lokaci guda a cikin ladabi da yanayin zafi da ƙwayoyin halitta da zafin jiki waɗanda zasu iya haifar da rashin lafiya. Bugu da ƙari, munanan haɗarurruka ga lafiya na iya fitowa a cikin wuraren da babu wadata da ingancin tsaftar jiki da muhalli, ayyukan likitanci ba a tsara su da kyau ba haka kuma babu tsaftataccen ruwa. Tafiya tsakanin ƙasashe na ƙaruwa, yadda ya zama miliyoyin mutane ke tafiya don aiki, zamantakewa, nishaɗi da kuma dalilai na aikin jin ƙaidalilai kowace shekara. Dole ne dukkanin matafiya su shirya don nau'ikan haɗari ga lafiya da za a iya samu cikin yanayin da ba a sani ba a kafin da yayin da kuma bayan tafiya. Ya kamata mutane da ke shirin tafiya su nemi shawara kan haɗarin da za su iya samu a wurin da zasu je kuma su fahimci hanyar da zasu kare lafiyarsu da kuma yadda zasu rage haɗarin kamuwa da cuta. Shirin gaba sune, matakan da suka dace da taka tsantsan na iya kare lafiyar matafiya, kuma suna rage haɗarin haɗari da samun cuta. Kodayake masana'antar likitanci da masana'antar tafiye-tafiye na iya samar da taimako mai yawa da kuma shawara kyau, ya kasance da alhakin matafiya ne neman bayanai da fahimtar haɗarurrukan da ke ƙunshe da kuma ɗaukan matakan kariya da suka dace don kiyaye lafiyarsu yayin tafiya. Haɗari: Yayin tantance haɗarurrukan lafiya na tafiya ƙasashen a duniya, ya kamata matafiya da ma'aikatan kiwon lafiya suyi la'akari da waɗannan manyan abubuwan da za a kawo:
translations.reference: Travel and health Overview: International travel can pose various risks to health, depending on the characteristics of both the traveller and the travel. Travellers may encounter sudden and significant changes in altitude, humidity, microbes, and temperature, which can result in ill-health. In addition, serious health risks may arise in areas where accommodation is of poor quality, hygiene and sanitation are inadequate, medical services are not well developed and clean water is unavailable. International travel is on the rise, as millions of people travel for professional, social, recreational and humanitarian purposes each year. All travellers must prepare for the variety of health risks they can be exposed to in unfamiliar environments before, during and after they travel. People planning to travel should seek advice on the potential hazards in their chosen destinations and understand how best to protect their health and minimize the risk of acquiring disease. Forward planning, appropriate preventive measures and careful precautions can protect the health of travellers, and minimize the risks of accident and of acquiring disease. Although the medical profession and the travel industry can provide extensive help and sound advice, it remains the traveller’s responsibility to seek information, to understand the risks involved and to take the necessary precautions to protect their health while travelling. Risk: When determining the health risks of international travel, travellers and health professionals should consider the following key factors:

**Safe Trip: Important Information: Traveling between countries can pose various health risks, depending on the environment you’re in. Travelers can experience significant changes in hospitality, temperature, exposure to pathogens, and physical discomfort, which can lead to illness. Furthermore, serious health hazards can arise in locations lacking adequate sanitation and environmental quality, with poorly designed healthcare services and a lack of clean water. International travel has increased, with millions of people traveling for work, social reasons, leisure, and to pursue educational opportunities each year. All travelers should prepare for the types of health risks that may be encountered during their trip, even those that are unforeseen. Individuals planning a trip should seek advice on the potential risks in the destination and understand how to protect their health and minimize the risk of infection.  Proactive measures include appropriate steps to safeguard traveler health and reduce the risk of illness. Although the medical and tourism industries can provide significant assistance and good advice, the responsibility ultimately lies with the traveler to gather information, understand the potential hazards, and take appropriate protective measures to maintain their health while traveling. Risks to Consider: When assessing health risks associated with international travel, travelers and healthcare professionals should consider the following key factors:**

translations.reference: environmental changes, such as altitude, humidity, temperature foodborne and waterborne diseases inadequate medical services injuries, including from road traffic accidents or recreational activities psychological health, including stress and culture shock Travellers may also be exposed to a number of infectious diseases, and many of these can be prevented by vaccination. Destinations where accommodation, hygiene and sanitation, medical care and water quality are of a high standard pose relatively few serious risks to the health of travellers. Where these services are not provided, travellers should take stringent precautions to avoid illness. Travel warnings from governmental sources should be taken seriously.

Boil drinking-water if safety is doubtful.

translations.source: Tarin fuka Taƙaitaccen bayani: Ƙwayar cuta mai suna Mycobacterium tuberculosis ce ke haifar da cutar tarin fuka, kuma mafi yawan lokaci tana shafar huhu. Tarin fuka na yaɗuwa ta hanyar iska idan mutane masu ƙwayar cutar a huhunsu suka yi tar, attishawa ko tofar da yawu. Mutum na buƙatar shaƙar ƙwayoyin cutar kaɗan ne kawai kafin ya kamu da ita. A kowace shekara, mutane miliyan 10 ne ke kamuwa da cutar tarin fuka(TB). Duk da kasancewarta cuta da za a iya karewa kuma ana iya warkewa, mutane miliyan 1.5 suna mutuwa daga tarin fuka a kowace shekara - wanda ya sa ta zama babbar cuta mai yaɗuwa da ke kisa a duniya. Tarin fuka na kan gaba a jerin sanadin mutuwar mutane masu cuta mai karyar garkuwar jiki HIV, kuma yana samar da kaso mai yawa na cutuka masu ƙin jin magani Yawancin masu kamuwa da cutar tarin fuka na rayuwa a ƙasashe masu ƙaranci da matsakaicin ƙarfin tattalin arziƙi, amma ana samun TB a ko'ina a duniya. Ana iya samun kusan rabin dukkan masu fama da tarin fuka a kasashe 8: Bangladesh, China, Indiya, Indonesiya, Najeriya, Pakistan, Philippines da Afirka ta Kudu. An ƙiyasta cewa kimanin kaso ɗaya bisa huɗu na al'ummar duniya na kamuwa da ƙwayar cutar TB, sai dai yawancin mutane ba za su kama cutar ta tarin fuka ba, wasu kuma jikinsu na yaƙar cutar.
translations.reference: Tuberculosis Overview: TB is caused by bacteria (Mycobacterium tuberculosis) and it most often affects the lungs. TB is spread through the air when people with lung TB cough, sneeze or spit. A person needs to inhale only a few germs to become infected. Every year, 10 million people fall ill with tuberculosis (TB). Despite being a preventable and curable disease, 1.5 million people die from TB each year – making it the world’s top infectious killer. TB is the leading cause of death of people with HIV and also a major contributor to antimicrobial resistance Most of the people who fall ill with TB live in low- and middle-income countries, but TB is present all over the world. About half of all people with TB can be found in 8 countries: Bangladesh, China, India, Indonesia, Nigeria, Pakistan, Philippines and South Africa. About a quarter of the global population is estimated to have been infected with TB bacteria, but most people will not go on to develop TB disease and some will clear the infection.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Hausa document into English.gs:

**Date of Document:  Information Report: Tuberculosis**

Tuberculosis (TB) is caused by the bacterium *Mycobacterium tuberculosis*, and most commonly affects the lungs. TB spreads through the air when people with TB disease cough, sneeze, or spit. A person only needs a small amount of the bacteria to become infected. Each year, 10 million people contract tuberculosis (TB). Despite being a preventable and treatable disease, 1.5 million people die from TB each year – which makes it a major, widespread disease that kills people around the world. TB is leading in the list of causes of death among people with HIV/AIDS, and it accounts for a significant proportion of drug-resistant diseases.  Most people with TB live in developing and middle-income countries, but TB is found everywhere in the world. Approximately half of all people with TB are found in eight countries: Bangladesh, China, India, Indonesia, Nigeria, Pakistan, the Philippines, and South Africa. It is estimated that about one in four people worldwide contract the TB bacteria, but most people do not develop the disease, while others’ bodies fight it off.

translations.source: Waɗanda ke ɗauke da ƙwayar cutar amma ba su kamu da rashin lafiya ba tukunna ba sa iya yaɗa ta. Mutanen da suka kamu da ƙwayar cutar tarin fuka na da hatsarin tsawon rayuwa na kaso 5 zuwa 10 na kamuwa da cutar ta TB. Mutanen da ke da raunin garkuwar jiki, kamar mutanen da ke rayuwa da cuta mai karya garkuwar jiki wato HIV, ƙaranci abinci mai gina jiki ko ciwon suga, ko masu shan sigari, sun fi zama cikin hatsarin yin rashin lafiyar. Alamomi: Alamomin cutar tarin fuka sun haɗa da: Taro na lokaci mai tsawo, Ciwon ƙirji, Kasala ko gajiya, Ramewa, Zazzaɓi, Gumin dare. Mafi yawan lokuta, waɗannan alamomi sukan kasance marasa tsanani na tsawon watanni, wanda hakan ke haifar da jinkiri wajen neman lafiya da kuma ƙara hatsarin yaɗa cutar ga wasu. Idan ma'aikacin lafiya ya yi zargin mara lafiya na fama a cutar tarin fuka, zai aika wannan marar lafiyar domin yin gwaji. Ta ɓangaren cutar tarin fuka da ta shafi huhu, za a buƙaci mara lafiya da ya ba da yawunsa don a yi gwajin cutar ta tarin fuka. Ga tarin fuka da bai shafi huhu ba kuma, ana iya gwada samfurin ruwan jiki ko ko wani ɓangare da cutar ta shafa. WHO na ba da shawara yin gwajin gano ƙwayoyin cuta cikin sauri a matsayin gwajin farko ga mutanen da ke nuna alamun kamuwa da tarin fuka. Sauran hanyoyin gwaje-gwajen na iya haɗawa da duba yawu a ƙarƙashin madubin binciken ƙwaƙwaf da kuma hoton ƙirji.
translations.reference: Those who are infected but not (yet) ill with the disease cannot transmit it. People infected with TB bacteria have a 5–10% lifetime risk of falling ill with TB. Those with compromised immune systems, such as people living with HIV, malnutrition or diabetes, or people who use tobacco, have a higher risk of falling ill. Symptoms: Common symptoms of TB disease include: Prolonged cough, Chest pain, Weakness or fatigue, Weight loss, Fever, Night sweats Often, these symptoms will be mild for many months, thus leading to delays in seeking care and increasing the risk of spreading the infection to others. If the healthcare provider suspects a patient to have TB disease, they will send the patient for testing. In the case of suspected lung TB disease, patients will be asked to give a sputum sample for testing for TB bacteria. For non-lung TB disease, samples of affected body fluids and tissue can be tested. WHO recommends rapid molecular diagnostic tests as initial tests for people showing signs and symptoms of TB. Other diagnostic tools can include sputum smear microscopy and chest X-rays.
translations.hypothesis:

Individuals with tuberculosis (TB) who do not present with signs of illness cannot transmit the disease. People infected with TB have a risk of developing a prolonged TB infection lasting 5 to 10 years. Individuals with conditions such as malnutrition, such as those living with HIV, malnutrition, or injuries, or those who smoke, are at higher risk of developing TB. Symptoms: The symptoms of TB include: rapid weight loss, diarrhea, abdominal pain or swelling, fever, cough, and night sweats. These symptoms often appear gradually over months, which can lead to delays in seeking medical attention and further increase the risk of transmission to others. If a healthcare worker suspects a patient has TB, they will report this illness for testing. For pulmonary TB, the patient will be required to provide a sputum sample for testing. For extra-pulmonary TB, a urine sample or other sample affected by the disease can be tested. WHO recommends rapid detection of TB bacteria as the initial test for individuals presenting with symptoms of TB infection. Other diagnostic methods may include examining stools under a microscope and chest X-rays.

translations.source: Wajen kamuwa da tarin fuka, mutum na gamuwa da ƙwayar cutar tarin fuka wacce ta zama a jiki ba tare da wani tasiri ba. Kamuwa da wannan ƙwayar cutar na iya juyewa ya zama cikakkiyar cutar tarin fuka idan garkuwar jikinsu ta samu rauni. Ta yiwu mutanen da suka kamu da ƙwayar cutar tarin fuka ba za su nuna wasu alamomin cutar ta tarin fuka ba. Don gano ko an kamu da cutar tarin fuka, ma'aikatan lafiya za su duba marasa lafiya da ke cikin haɗari don kawar da yiwuwar kamuwa da tarin fuka, kuma suna iya amfani da gwajin fata ko jini don bincikar tarin fuka. Magani: Tarin fuka cuta ce da ake iya warkewa. Ana magance ta ta hanyar shan daidaitattun magunguna kala 4 na tsawon wata 6. Magungunan da ake yawan amfani da su sun haɗa da rifampicin da isoniazid. Amma wasu lokutan cutar tarin fuka ba ta karɓar daidaitattun magungunan. A wannan yanayin, mara lafiyan yana fama da tarin fuka da ba ya jin magani. Tarin fuka da ba ya jin magani na ɗaukar lokaci mai tsawo kuma yana da sarƙaƙiya.
translations.reference: With TB infection, a person gets infected with TB bacteria that lie inactive in the body. This infection can develop into TB disease if their immune system weakens. People with TB infection do not show any signs or symptoms of TB. To identify TB infection, healthcare providers will screen at-risk patients to rule out active TB, and they may use a skin or blood test to check for TB infection. Treatment: TB disease is curable. It is treated by standard 6 month course of 4 antibiotics. Common drugs include rifampicin and isoniazid. In some cases the TB bacteria does not respond to the standard drugs. In this case, the patient has drug-resistant TB. for drug-resistant TB is longer and more complex.

“Upon experiencing a series of headaches, a person may develop a latent form of tuberculosis that remains within the body without any noticeable effect. Exposure to this latent form can escalate and become full-blown tuberculosis if the body’s defenses are compromised.  Individuals with latent tuberculosis may not show any signs of the disease. To determine if someone has latent tuberculosis, healthcare professionals will examine individuals at risk for the possibility of contracting tuberculosis, and they can use chest X-rays or blood tests to detect tuberculosis.

Treatment: Tuberculosis is a curable disease. It is treated by taking a regimen of four specific medications for six weeks. The medications commonly used include rifampicin and isoniazid. However, in some cases, tuberculosis does not respond to standard treatment. In this situation, a patient with undiagnosed tuberculosis continues to suffer from tuberculosis without treatment and develops a shorter, more severe form.”

---

*   “Ƙwayar cutar tarin fuka” (tuberculosis) is consistently translated as “tuberculosis.”
*   “Marasa lafiya da ke cikin haɗari” (at risk individuals) was translated as “individuals at risk.”

translations.source: Ana ba wa marar lafiyar maganin tarin fuka da yake buƙata tare da bayanai da kuma sa'ido da tallafi daga ma'aikacin lafiya ko wani mai aikin sa kai da aka horas. Idan babu tallafi, tabbatar da an kammala shan maganin na iya zama abu mai wahala. Idan ba a kammala shan maganin yadda ya kamata ba, cutar na iya juyewa zuwa wacce ba ta jin magani kuma ta yaɗu. Game da kamuwa da ƙwayar cutar tarin fuka kuwa (ta yadda mara lafiyar ya kamu da ƙwayar cutar amma bai fara rashin lafiya ba), ana iya ba shi maganin kariya daga cutar tarin fuka don hana bayyanar alamomin cutar. Maganin iri ɗaya ake sha da na magance cutar, amma na lokacin da bai kai wancan tsawo ba. Zaɓuɓɓukan magani na baya-bayan nan sun ƙara gajarce tsawon lokacin shan maganin zuwa wata 1 ko 3 kawai, idan aka kwatanta da watanni 6 da akan yi a baya.
translations.reference: The course of TB drugs is provided to the patient with information, supervision and support by a health worker or trained volunteer. Without such support, treatment adherence can be difficult. If the treatment is not properly completed, the disease can become drug-resistant and can spread. In the case of TB infection (where the patient is infected with TB bacteria but not ill), TB preventive treatment can be given to stop the onset of disease. This treatment uses the same drugs for a shorter time. Recent treatment options have shortened the duration to treatment to only 1 or 3 months, as compared to 6 months in the past.

Previous treatment options have increased the duration of treatment to 1 or 3 weeks, compared to 6 months or less.”

translations.source: Hasken Ultraviolet radiation Taƙaitaccen bayani: Ultraviolet radiation (UV) na nufin hasken da ya faɗo a ma'aunin haske na 100-400nm, wanda ya fi ƙarfi kuma yake da ƙarancin nisan zango fiye da da hasken da ake iya gani da ido. UV radiation na samuwa ne daga rana, amma kuma ana iya ƙirƙirarsa ta hanyoyin da ɗan adam ya samar waɗanda ake amfani da su a masana'antu, kasuwanci da motsa jiki. Yankin UV ya ƙunshi ma'aunin haske 100-400 nm kuma an raba shi zuwa rukuni uku: UVA (315-400 nm) UVB (280-315 nm) UVC (100-280 nm). Yayin da hasken rana ke wuce sararin samaniya, shimfiɗar sama, tururin ruwa, iskar oxygen da ta carbon dioxide na shanye duk hasken da ke rukunin UVC da kuma kaso 90% na wanda ke rukunin UVB. Hasken rukunin UVA bai cika tasirantuwa da sinadaran da ke sararin samaniya ba. Dun haka, hasken UV da ke isowa duniya yawanci ya ƙunshi UVA ne da kaɗan daga UVB. Adadin hasken UV radiation daga rana da ke isowa duniya ya dogara da abubuwa da yawa, ciki har da tsayin rana a sararin sama, nisa ta ɓangaren latitude, kaurin gajimare, nisan altitude, kaurin shimfiɗar sararin samaniya ta ozone da yanayin ƙasa. Rage kauri da shimfiɗar sararin samaniya ta ozone ta yi saboda gurɓacewar yanayi da ɗan adam ya haifar yana ƙara adadin UVA da UVB waɗanda ke isowa duniya. Wannan na iya yin tasiri ga lafiyar ɗan adam, dabbobi, halittun ruwa da rayuwar shuka.
translations.reference: Ultraviolet radiation Overview: Ultraviolet (UV) radiation covers the wavelength range of 100–400 nm, which is a higher frequency and lower wavelength than visible light. UV radiation comes naturally from the sun, but it can also be created by artificial sources used in industry, commerce and recreation. The UV region covers the wavelength range 100-400 nm and is divided into three bands: UVA (315-400 nm)UVB (280-315 nm)UVC (100-280 nm). As sunlight passes through the atmosphere, all UVC and approximately 90% of UVB radiation is absorbed by ozone, water vapour, oxygen and carbon dioxide. UVA radiation is less affected by the atmosphere. Therefore, the UV radiation reaching the Earth’s surface is largely composed of UVA with a small UVB component. The amount of UV radiation from the sun that hits the Earth’s surface depends on several factors, including the sun’s height in the sky, latitude, cloud cover, altitude, the thickness of the ozone layer and ground reflection. Reductions in the ozone layer due to human-created pollution increase the amount of UVA and UVB that reaches the surface. This can impact human health, animals, marine organisms and plant life.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's the English translation of the Hausa document, aiming for clarity and accuracy:

**The Effects of Ultraviolet Radiation: Recent Information**

Ultraviolet radiation (UV) refers to light within the wavelength range of 100-400nm, which is more intense and has a shorter wavelength than visible light. UV radiation is available from the sun, but it can also be generated by human-made methods used in industry, commerce, and recreation. The UV spectrum includes the wavelength range of 100-400 nm and is divided into three categories: UVA (315-400 nm), UVB (280-315 nm), and UVC (100-280 nm). As sunlight passes through the atmosphere, cloud cover, the presence of water vapor, oxygen, and carbon dioxide all filter out much of the UVC range and approximately 90% of the UVB range. UVA light does not significantly penetrate the atmosphere.  Consequently, the sunlight reaching the Earth primarily consists of UVA with a small amount of UVB. The amount of UV radiation from the sun reaching the Earth depends on several factors, including the length of daylight in the atmosphere, latitude, the angle of incidence, altitude, the thickness of the ozone layer, and the environmental conditions.  A reduction in the thickness of the ozone layer is occurring due to environmental pollution and human-induced factors, which increases the amount of UVA and UVB reaching the Earth. This can have an impact on human health, animal health, aquatic ecosystems, and animal life.

---

**Notes on the Translation:**

*   I've aimed for a natural-sounding English translation while maintaining the original meaning.
*   I've used technical terms like "wavelength" and "atmosphere" to accurately convey the scientific concepts.
*   I've tried to keep the language clear and accessible.

Would you like me to refine any part of the translation, or perhaps translate a different document?

translations.source: A cikin mutane, ƙari a haske UV na iya haifar da ciwon sankarar fata, amosanin ido da karya garkuwar jiki. Kariya: ƙari a yawan masu kamuwa da cutar kansar fata a cikin gomman shekarun da suka gabata yana da alaƙa mai ƙarfi da ƙarin shiga rana yayin ayyukan waje da kuma hanyoyin samar da hasken UV da ɗan adam ya samar kamar fitilu masu haske kamar rana da gadajen sauya launin fata. Har ila yau, amfani da ya wuce gona da iri shi ne sanadin cutar da idanu da gaskuwar jiki. Ɗaukar wasu 'yan matakan na kiyayewa na iya rage hatsarin kamuwa da illolin na lafiya: Iyakance lokaci shiga tsakar rana daga 10 na safe zuwa 4 na yamma, musamman a ranakun da ma'aunin UV ya yi sama Lokacin da hasken UV ya fi tsanani kuma dole sai ka fita waje, yi ƙoƙari ku nemo gilashin kare rana kuma ka sanya tufafin kariya, gami da hula mai malafa da za ta ba da kariya daga rana ga kai, fuska da wuyanka. Yi amfani da man shiga rana na aƙalla ajin 15 SPF - ko wanda ya fi shi - kuma a sake shafa bayan duk awa 2. Gujewa amfani da fitilu masu haske irin na rana da wuraren sauya launin fata, domin an san suna lalata fata da idanu. A taƙaita shigar yara hasken UV saboda suna wani mataki mai muhimmanci na girma don haka sun fi manya saurin fuskantar matsala daga ababe masu hatsari a muhalli. Gaɓoɓi masu muhimmanci, kamar garkuwar jikin ɗan adam ba su kammalu ba yayin da ake haihuwar mutum, don haka muhalli mara aminci na iya kawo cikas a ci gabansu kamar yadda ya dace. Martani daga Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya: Hukumar na aiki da ƙasashen da ke ƙarƙashinta da kuma abokan hulɗarta don ƙara wayar da kai game da hatsarin da ke tattare da yawan ta'ammali da haske ultraviolet radiation.
translations.reference: In humans, increased UV exposure can cause skin cancers, cataracts and immune system damage. Prevention: The rise in the number of cases of skin cancer over the past decades is strongly related to increased exposure to the sun during outdoor activities and to artificial sources of UV radiation such as sunlamps and tanning beds. Overexposure is also the underlying cause of harmful effects on the eyes and immune system. Adopting a few simple precautions can greatly reduce the risk of these health conditions: Limit time in the midday sun from 10 a.m. to 4 p.m., particularly on days when the UV index is high When UV rays are most intense and you must be outside, try to find shade and wear protective clothing, including a brimmed hat that provides sun protection for your head, face and neck. Use a broad-spectrum sunscreen of at least 15 SPF – ideally higher– and reapply every 2 hours. Avoid sunlamps and tanning parlours, as they are known to damage the skin and eyes. Children’s UV exposure should be carefully limited because they are in a dynamic state of growth and therefore more susceptible to environmental threats than adults. Many vital functions such as the immune system are not fully developed at birth, and unsafe environments may interfere with their normal development. WHO Response: WHO works with Member States and partners to increase public understanding of the effects of overexposure to ultraviolet radiation.

translations.source: Shirin INTERSUN shiri ne na haɗin gwiwa tsakanin Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya, Shirin Muhalli na Majalisar Ɗinkin Duniya, Ƙungiyar Lura da Sararin Samaniya ta World Meteorological Organization, cibiyar binciken da ya shafi cutar sankara ta International Agency on Cancer Research da kuma hukumar International Commission on Non-Ionizing Radiation Protection. Yana ƙarfafawa da tantance bincike a kan UV radiation ga lafiya, kuma yana samar da hanyoyin agaji da suka dace ta hanyar samar da jagora, shawarwari da yaɗa bayanai. Manufar shirin ta haɗa da bayar da shawarwari masu inganci kan illar da hasken UV ke da shi ga lafiya da muhalli, karfafa gwiwar ƙasashe su ɗauki matakin rage illa ga lafiya da UV ke haifarwa da kuma samar da tsari ga mahukuntan ƙasa game da shirye-shiryen wayar da kan hasken rana. Don cimma wannan manufar, shirin yana haɗa gwiwa da masana da ƙwararrun hukumomi wajen aiwatar da muhimman ayyukan bincike, ganowa da ƙididdige haɗarin UV radiation ga lafiya, samar da ingantaccen hasashe game da illar da ke tattare da canji a yawan ta'ammali da hasken UV ga lafiya da muhalli, da lalata sararin samaniya, da samar da hanyoyin da za a iya amfani da su wajen saka ido kan canje-canje masu tasiri kan lafiya da hasken UV ke haifarwa bayan tsawon lokaci. Shirin yana samar da kayan aiki da shawarwari da suka danganci ma'aunin UV, kariya daga hasken rana ga yara, gadaje masu fitar da haske irin na rana, yawon shaƙatawa da tabbatar da lafiya yayin gudanar aiki ko sana'a.
translations.reference: The INTERSUN Programme is a collaboration between WHO, the United Nations Environment Programme, the World Meteorological Organization, the International Agency on Cancer Research and the International Commission on Non-Ionizing Radiation Protection. It promotes and evaluates research on the health effects of UV radiation, and develops an appropriate response through guidelines, recommendations and information dissemination. The goals of the programme including providing practical and sound advice on the health and environmental impacts of UV exposure, to encourage countries to take action to reduce UV-induced health risks and to provide guidance to national authorities about sun awareness programmes. To this end, the programme collaborates with experts and specialist agencies to implement key research activities, identifies and quantifies health risks from UV radiation, develops reliable predictions of health and environmental consequences of changes in UV exposure with stratospheric ozone depletion, and develops practical ways of monitoring change in UV-induced health effects over time. The programme provides tools and guidelines related to the UV index, sun protection for children, artificial sunbeds, tourism and occupational health.

INTERSUN is a collaborative program between the World Health Organization, the United Nations Environment Programme, the World Meteorological Organization’s Expert Team on Ultraviolet Radiation, the International Agency for Research on Cancer, and the International Commission on Non-Ionizing Radiation Protection. It provides guidance and standards for research on the effects of UV radiation on health, and offers appropriate measures through the provision of indicators, recommendations, and data dissemination. The program’s purpose is to provide high-quality advice on the harms of UV radiation to health and the environment, strengthen countries’ ability to take steps to reduce the health impacts of UV radiation, and provide frameworks to national authorities regarding UV protection programs. To achieve this goal, the program collaborates with experts and relevant authorities to implement key research activities, monitor and assess the risk of UV radiation to health, develop accurate data on the changes in UV radiation levels affecting health and the environment, and mitigate environmental damage, as well as provide tools to monitor changes with significant impacts on health and UV radiation. The program provides tools and recommendations related to UV index values, protection from sunlight for children, sunshades that emit sunlight like the sun, recreational activities and ensuring health while working or engaging in professions.

translations.source: Gamammen tsarin kula da lafiya a faɗin duniya wato Universal Health Coverage Taƙaitaccen bayani: Universal Health Coverage (UHC) wato samar da gamammen tsarin kula da lafiya a faɗin duniya na nufin cewa kowanne mutum na da damar amfani da cikakku kuma ingantattun ayyukan lafiya da suke buƙata, a lokacin da suke buƙata kuma a wajen da suke buƙatarsu, ba tare da matsalar rashin kuɗi ba. Ya haɗa da cikakkun ayyukan lafiya na tilas, daga wayar da kai zuwa kariya, magance cuta, gyaran hali da kula da masu matsanantan cututtuka. Domin cika wannan alƙawarin, akwai buƙatar ƙasashe su kasance suna da tsarin kiwon lafiya da yake da ƙarfi, yake aiki kuma suke aiki ga kowa ba tare da wariya ba, masu tushe a cikin al'ummar da suke yi wa aiki. Kula da lafiya matakin farko ne hanya mafi aiki kuma mai arha wajen samar da wannan tsari na kiwon lafiya. Kowace ƙasa na da hanya ta daban ta cimma gamammen tsarin kula da lafiya su kuma yanke shawarar abin da za su yi ta la'akari da buƙatun al'ummarsu da kuma kayan aikin da suke da su. Sanya jari a Matakin Farko na Kula da Lafiya na tabbatar da cewa an gane dukkan waɗannan buƙatu, an ba su muhimmanci kuma an warware su ta hanyar da ta haɗa da kowane ɓangare; an samar da ma'aikatan lafiya wadatattu masu sanin makamar aiki; kuma dukkan ɓangarori a al'umma suna ba da gudummuwa wajen fuskantar ƙalubalen muhalli da tattalin arziƙi da ke shafar lafiya da walwala, wanda ya haɗa da shirya wa, kai agaji da kuma farfaɗowa da buƙatun lafiya da kan taso cikin gaggawa. Martanin Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya: A matsayin wani tushe na samar da Gamammen tsarin kula da lafiya (UHC) Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya na ba da shawarar sake saita tsare-tsaren kula da lafiya ta amfani da tsarin kula da lafiya a matakin farko (PHC). Tabbatar da samuwar gamammen tsarin kula da lafiya (UHC) shiri ne mai muhimmanci na Hukumar Kula da Lafiya ta duniya, da burin samun ƙarin mutane biliyan 1 da za su ci moriyar tsarin kula da lafiya na duniya a shekarar 2025. A ƙasashe masu tsarin kula da lafiya mai rauni, muna mayar da hankali kan samar da taimakon dabarun aiki don gina hukumomi na ƙasa da ayyukan da za su cike muhimman gurabe a lokacin buƙatun gaggawa.
translations.reference: Universal health coverage Overview: Universal health coverage (UHC) means that all people have access to the full range of quality health services they need, when and where they need them, without financial hardship. It covers the full continuum of essential health services, from health promotion to prevention, treatment, rehabilitation and palliative care. To deliver on this promise, countries need to have strong, efficient and equitable health systems that are rooted in the communities they serve. Primary health care (PHC) is the most effective and cost-efficient way to get there. Every country has a different path to achieving UHC and to decide what to cover based on the needs of their populations and the resources at hand. Investing in PHC ensures that all those needs are identified, prioritized and addressed in an integrated way; that there is a robust and equipped health and care workforce; and that all sectors of society contribute to confronting the environmental and socio-economic factors that affect health and well-being, including preparing for, responding to and recovering from emergencies. WHO response: As a foundation for UHC, WHO recommends reorienting health systems using a primary health care (PHC) approach. Achieving UHC is a WHO strategic priority, with the goal of 1 billion more people benefitting from universal health coverage by 2025. In countries with fragile health systems, we focus on technical assistance to build national institutions and service delivery to fill critical gaps in emergencies.

**Global Health Coverage Strategy: Universal Health Coverage – A Summary**

Universal Health Coverage (UHC) – meaning global health coverage – means that every person has access to complete and quality health services, when and where they need them, without financial hardship. This includes a full range of essential health services, from primary care to disease prevention, treatment, rehabilitation, and palliative care. 

To achieve this goal, countries need strong, effective, and equitable health systems that serve everyone without discrimination. Primary healthcare is the most effective and affordable way to deliver this health coverage. Each country has a different way to achieve universal health coverage and decides based on the needs of its community and the resources available.

Investing in Primary Healthcare is crucial to meet these needs, ensuring qualified healthcare professionals are available, training them effectively, and involving all parts of the community in addressing environmental, economic, and social challenges that impact health and well-being. This includes preparedness, response, and mitigation of emerging health needs.

**World Health Organization’s Response:**

As a key driver of Universal Health Coverage (UHC), the World Health Organization recommends strengthening health systems through primary healthcare (PHC). Ensuring universal health coverage (UHC) is a key priority for the World Health Organization, with the goal of reaching 1 billion more people with essential health services by 2025. In countries with weak health systems, the focus is on developing strategies to build national health authorities and programs to address critical gaps in emergency care. 

---

**Notes on the Translation:**

*   I've aimed for a clear and accessible English translation, suitable for a general audience.
*   I've tried to maintain the original meaning and intent of the Hausa text.
*   “Gamammen tsarin kula da lafiya” is consistently translated as “Universal Health Coverage” (UHC).
*   “Matakin farko” is consistently translated as “Primary Healthcare” (PHC).

Would you like me to refine any part of the translation or provide a different version (e.g., more formal or simplified)?

translations.source: A wuraren da ke da tsarin kula da lafiya tsayayye, muna ƙarfafar samar da tasirin kula da lafiyar al'umma ta ɓangaren faɗaɗa aikin ga kowa ta hanyar samar da dokiki don samar da tsare-tsaren da za a ci moriya nan gaba da kuma ba da muhimmin tallafi wajen inganta aiki. Wannan aiki ana yinsa bisa bin tsarin aiwatarwa da aka saba da kuma yarjejeniyoyi; bayanai, bincike da ƙirƙira; da shugabanci a fagen diflomasiyya, wayar da kai, daidaiton jinsi, wadatar kula da lafiya da haƙƙoƙin al'umma, ayyukan da suka shafi ɓangarori da dama, da kuma hada-hadar kuɗi. Ajandar 2030 ta aikace-aikacen Hukumar Kula da Lafiya ta Duniya ta dace da Manufofin Ci gaba Mai Ɗorewa (SDG'S) na 3.8 wanda yake mayar da hankali wajen samar da gamammen tsarin kula da lafiya a duniya, wanda ya haɗa da ba da kariya ga marasa ƙarfin tattalin arziƙi, damar amfani da ingantattun ayyukan kula da lafiya da kuma damar amfani da magangunan da alluran rigakafi da ake buƙata masu aminci, da suke aiki, ingantattu kuma masu sauƙin saye ga kowa. A duniya bakiɗaya, ci gaba a fannin samar da gamammen tsarin kula da lafiya abu ne da ke fuskantar ƙalubale. Ci gaba ta fannin adadin mutanen da ke amfana da ayyukan lafiya ya samu tsaiko yayin da adadin al'ummar da ke fuskantar mummunan kashe kuɗi don neman lafiya ke ci gaba da ƙaruwa. Hukumar Lafiya ta duniya ta ci gaba da sa ido kan ci gaban tana kuma wallafa rahoto game da halin da duniya ke ciki don bayyana shaida mafi sabunta bayan duk shekara biyu. Duba rahotannin a nan kuma ka ziyarci shafin sahihin bayani game da Gamammen Tsarin Kula da lafiya (UHC) don ƙarin bayani.
translations.reference: In more robust health system settings, we drive public health impact towards health coverage for all through policy dialogue for the systems of the future and strategic support to improve performance. This work is supported by normative guidance and agreements; data, research and innovation; and leadership in the realms of diplomacy, advocacy, gender equality, health equity and human rights, multisectoral action, and finance. 2030 Agenda for SDGs WHO’s work is aligned with the Sustainable Development Goals (SDG) target 3.8, which focuses on achieving universal health coverage, including financial risk protection, access to quality essential health-care services and access to safe, effective, quality and affordable essential medicines and vaccines for all. Globally, progress towards universal health coverage is a challenge. Progress in service coverage has stalled while the proportion of the population facing catastrophic out-of-pocket health spending increases continuously. WHO monitors progress and publishes global reports to reveal the latest evidence every two years. See the reports here and visit the UHC fact sheet for more information.
