“Regarding locations with comprehensive health systems, we are progressively creating an impact on community health by ensuring access to essential medicines for everyone through the development of prioritized plans and providing crucial support to improve performance. This work is carried out in accordance with established implementation frameworks and agreements; involving data collection, research, and innovation, as well as diplomacy, communication, gender equality, integrated health and human rights, and activities across various sectors, including resource mobilization and integration. The 2030 Agenda aligns with the World Health Organization’s Sustainable Development Goal 3.8, which focuses on establishing universal health coverage, including protection for vulnerable populations, access to quality health services, and access to safe, effective, affordable, and available health commodities. Globally, progress in establishing universal health coverage faces challenges. The number of people benefiting from health services continues to grow while the number of communities facing severe health burdens increases. The World Health Organization continues to monitor progress and disseminate reports on the global situation every two years, providing the latest updates. See also the reports here and visit the official website for more information on Universal Health Coverage (UHC).”

translations.source: Kula da lafiya a birane Taƙaitaccen bayani: Faɗaɗar birani na ɗaya daga cikin ababen na kan gaba da ke tashe a duniya a ƙarni na 21 wanda ke da tasiri sosai a kan lafiya. Sama sa kaso 55 da al'ummar duniya na rayuwa a birane - ana sa rai wannan adadin zai ƙaru zuwa kaso 68 nan da 2050. Da yake faɗaɗa birane da zai zo nan gaba zai auku ne a ƙasashe masu tasowa, duniyar a yau tana da wata dama ta musamman ta tsara yadda faɗaɗar biranen za ta kasance da ma sauran manyan ababen da suka shafi ci gaban birane a wata hanya da za ta ba da kariya ta kuma ƙarfafi kiwon lafiya. Wannan yana da muhimmanci, musamman da yake lafiya da walwalar al'umma na iya zama kadara mafi daraja ta kowanne birni. Sai dai, da yawa daga cikin mutane biliyan 4.2 da ke rayuwa a birane na fama da matsalar ƙarancin muhalli da hanyoyin sufuri, rashin tsaftar muhalli da sarrafa shara, da kuma ingancin iskar shaƙa wanda bai cika sharuɗɗan da hukumar lafiya ta duniya ta bayar ba. Sauran nau'ukan gurɓatar yanayi, kamar su hayaniya, ruwa da guɓacewar ƙasa, da wuraren da ake kira tsibiran zafi na birane, da rashin sarari don yin tafiyar ƙafa, tuƙa keke da kuma rayuwa mai cike da kuzari sun haɗu sun sanya biranen zama cibiyar annobar cutukan da ba sa yaɗuwa da kuma haddasa sauyin yanayi. Ma'ajiyar Bayanai ta Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya game da Kiwon Lafiya a Birane Tasiri: Kusan kaso 40 na faɗaɗar birane na faruwa ne a unguwannin talakawa da ba su da ruwa mai tsafta da tsaftar muhalli, kuma kaso 91 na mutanen da ke zaune a birane na shaƙar gurɓatacciyar iska. Idan ana batun abinci mai lafiya, faɗaɗar birane na ƙara nesanta mutane da gonaki wanda ke ƙara ƙarfafar buƙatar abincin da aka sarrafa wanda ba shi da kyau ga jiki.
translations.reference: Urban health Overview: Urbanization is one of the leading global trends of the 21st century that has a significant impact on health. Over 55% of the world’s population live in urban areas – a proportion that is expected to increase to 68% by 2050. As most future urban growth will take place in developing countries, the world today has a unique opportunity to guide urbanization and other major urban development trends in a way that protects and promotes health. This is important, not least because the health and well-being of citizens is perhaps a city’s most important asset. However, most of the 4.2 billion people living in cities suffer inadequate housing and transport, poor sanitation and waste management, and air quality that fails WHO guidelines. Other forms of pollution, such as noise, water and soil contamination, so-called urban heat islands, and a lack of space for walking, cycling and active living further combine to make cities epicentres of a noncommunicable disease epidemic and drivers of climate change. WHO Repository on Urban Health Impact: Around 40% of urban growth is in slums that lack safe water and sanitation, and 91% of people in urban areas breathe polluted air. When it comes to healthy diets, urbanization increases the distance from farm to fork, driving demand for unhealthy, processed foods.

**Maintaining Health in Cities**

**A Recent Report: Urban expansion is one of the leading global trends of the 21st century, significantly impacting health. Approximately 55% of the world’s population now lives in cities, and this number is projected to rise to 68% by 2050. As cities expand, it’s crucial to plan for this growth in a way that provides protection and strengthens healthcare systems. This is particularly important, given that the health and well-being of a city’s population can be the most significant priority. However, many of the 4.2 billion people living in cities face challenges such as environmental pollution, inadequate transportation, poor air quality, and unreliable electricity supply, all of which fail to meet the standards set by the World Health Organization.**

**Furthermore, other environmental pollutants, including noise, water contamination, and land degradation, along with heat islands within cities and a lack of resources for physical activity (such as walking, cycling, and maintaining a healthy lifestyle), contribute to making cities centers for the spread of infectious diseases and exacerbating climate change. **

**Data from the World Health Organization on Health in Cities: Approximately 40% of urban expansion occurs in low-income areas lacking access to clean water and sanitation, and 91% of urban residents breathe polluted air. Regarding food security, urban expansion increases the number of people and plots of land needing food production, which puts a strain on the body and is not beneficial to health.**

---

translations.source: Mazauna birane suna cikin hatsarin fuskantar abubuwan da sauyin yanayi ke haifar wa saboda sun dogara da makamashi mai ƙonewa wajen sufuri, girki da ɗumama gidaje. Birane ne ke da alhakin samar fiye da kaso biyu bisa uku na makamashi a duniya kuma suke fitar da kaso 60 na gurɓatacciyar iskar gas, kuma waɗanda ke birane na iya fuskantar yanayin da ya fi na ƙauyukan da ke kewaye da su tsauri da digiri 3-5 saboda yaɗuwar gine-gine da ƙarancin buɗaɗɗun wurare masu bishiyoyi. Dukkan waɗannan na saka mazauna birane cikin hatsari ta fuskar lafiya. Yawancin sabubban mutuwa na kan gaba guda 10 na da alaƙa ta kusa da faɗaɗar birane cikin sauri kuma ba bisa tsari ba, da kuma tsarawa da shirya birane ba yadda ya kamata ba. Biranen suna fuskantar ninki uku na hatsarin yaɗuwar cututtuka kamar HIV/AIDS, tarin fuka, limoniya, zazzaɓin dengue da gudawa; da cututtuka marasa yaɗuwa kamar cututtukan zuciya, bugun jini, asma, kansa, ciwon sukari da damuwa; da kuma tashin hankali da raunuka, ciki har da rauni da hatsarin ababen hawa. Martani daga Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya: Duk da ƙalubalen da suke fuskanta, biranen na iya samar da damammaki don ingantacciyar lafiya, tsabtataccen muhalli da ayyukan da suka shafi inganta yanayi. Dole ne dokoki masu ƙarfi na birane su ba da fifiko kan kiwon lafiya, saboda tana da mahimmanci wajen samar da rayuwa mai kyau a birane, samar da ma'aikata masu ƙwazo, samar da al'ummomin masu juriya da karsashi, ba da damar tafiye-tafiye, haɓaka hulɗar zamantakewa da kare al'umma masu rauni. Tsarin Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya game da lafiyar birane ya mayar da hankali kan samun ingantacciyar iska, ruwa da muhalli mai tsafta, da sauran abubuwan da ke tabbatar da muhalli; lafiyayyen tsarin birane; muhalli mai cike da ƙoshin lafiya kuma mara hayaƙi; tafiye-tafiye cikin aminci da lafiya; hana aukuwar tashin hankali da raunuka; goyon bayan ingantaccen tsarin samar da abinci; kula da muhalli ta fannin sa ido kan cututtukan da ƙwari masu shan jini ke yaɗawa; da kuma shirya birane wajen fuskantar buƙatun gaggawa.
translations.reference: Urban dwellers are also highly vulnerable to the effects of climate change because of their dependence on fossil fuels for transport, cooking and heating. Cities account for over two thirds of the world’s energy and emit 60% of greenhouse gases, and those inland may experience temperatures 3–5º C higher than surrounding rural areas because of their large expanses of concrete and limited open green spaces. All of this puts urban dwellers’ health at risk. Most the top 10 causes of death are closely related to rapid and unplanned urbanization, and poor urban design and planning. Cities face the triple health burden of infectious diseases like HIV/AIDS, tuberculosis, pneumonia, dengue and diarrhoea; noncommunicable diseases like heart disease, stroke, asthma, cancer, diabetes and depression; and violence and injuries, including road traffic injuries. WHO response: Despite their challenges, cities can create opportunities for better health, cleaner environments and climate action. Strong urban policies must prioritize health, as it is essential for fostering good urban livelihoods, building a productive workforce, creating resilient and vibrant communities, enabling mobility, promoting social interaction and protecting vulnerable populations. WHO’s approach to urban health focuses on better air quality, water and sanitation, and other environmental determinants; healthy urban planning; healthier and smoke-free environments; safe and healthy mobility; preventing violence and injuries; promoting healthy food systems and diets; environmental management of vector-borne diseases; and urban preparedness for emergencies.

**Urban areas face significant risks due to their reliance on fossil fuels for transportation, building construction, and energy supply. Cities are responsible for over two-thirds of global energy consumption and emit 60% of global air pollution, and urban dwellers experience environmental conditions up to 3-5 degrees higher than those in areas surrounding them due to urbanization and a lack of open spaces.**

**All of these factors put urban residents at risk of various health problems. The rate of new deaths is closely linked to the rapid expansion of cities. Furthermore, cities face challenges in urban planning and infrastructure development. Cities account for approximately three-quarters of infectious diseases such as HIV/AIDS, tuberculosis, influenza, dengue fever, and malaria; as well as non-communicable diseases such as heart disease, stroke, asthma, diabetes, mental health disorders, and injuries; and also face risks of violence and substance abuse.**

**Response from the World Health Organization: Despite these challenges, cities can create opportunities for improved health, environmental sustainability, and climate action. Cities need strong regulations to prioritize health, as it is crucial for providing a good quality of life, creating a skilled workforce, fostering resilient and cohesive communities, enabling tourism, promoting social cohesion, and protecting vulnerable populations. The World Health Organization’s framework on urban health focuses on achieving clean air, water, and a healthy environment; strengthening urban health systems; creating a safe and peaceful environment; enabling safe and accessible mobility; preventing mental health crises and substance abuse; supporting sustainable food production; monitoring environmental risks related to vector-borne diseases; and preparing cities to respond to emergencies.**

translations.source: Yin aiki a sassa daban-daban da kuma tabbatar da haɗin kai na manufofi a wurare daban-daban na da muhimmanci wajen samar da muhalli mai cike goyon baya da kuma samar da kyakkyawan yanayi don kiwon lafiya, wanda ke tabbatar da cewa an yi la'akari da lafiya da adalci a wajen tsarawa, zuba jari da yanke shawara a matakin gida. Don taimakawa ƙasashen da ke ƙarƙashinta su magance waɗannan abubuwa masu muhimmanci da ke sama, Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya tana aiki don ƙarfafa tabbataccen tsari mai tushe don taimakawa mahukunta su yanke shawara cike da ilimi yayin magance matsalolin lafiya. Yana ba da kayan aiki da ba da shawara kan abin da ke aiki, kuma yana goyan bayan sa ido kan muhimman alamun da ke da alaƙa da lafiya. Hukumar lafiya ta duniya ta jagoranci da ɗabbaƙa tsarin inganta mu'amala tsakanin birni-da-birni da kuma taimakawa wajen samar da cibiyoyi da tsare-tsaren aiwatarwa don kyakkyawan shugabanci don lafiya da walwala a birane.
translations.reference: Working across sectors and ensuring the coherence of policies across different areas is key to creating supportive and enabling environments for health, which ensures that health and equity considerations are integrated throughout the planning process, investments and policy decisions at the local level. To help Member States address the above priorities, WHO works to strengthen the evidence base to help policy-makers make informed decisions when addressing health risks. It provides tools and guidance on what works, and supports monitoring of key health-related indicators. WHO leads and engages in fostering city-to-city exchanges and helps develop institutional and policy frameworks for good urban governance for health and well-being in cities.

It provides tools and advice on what is working and monitors key indicators related to health. The World Health Organization leads and facilitates collaboration between cities and helps to establish implementation centers and strategies for effective leadership in health and well-being in urban areas.”

translations.source: Cin zarafin yara Taƙaitaccen bayani: Cin zarafin yara ya ƙunshi duk wani nau'i na cin zarafi da ake yi wa mutane 'yan ƙasa da shekara 18. Ga jarirai da ƙananan yara, yawanci cin zarafi na ƙunsar musgunawa yara (watau cin zarafi da ya shafi duka a jiki, lalata da rashin kulawa) daga iyaye da sauran masu iko. Yara maza da mata na fuskantar barazana iri ɗaya ta cin zarafi na zahiri da sauya tunani, da rashin kula, kuma yara mata sun fi fuskantar barazanar cin zarafi da ya shafi lalata. Yayin da yara ke kaiwa matakin samartaka, cin zarafi tsakanin tsararraki da abokan hulɗa na kusa, ƙari a kan cin zarafin yara na ƙara yaɗuwa sosai. Ana iya hana aukuwar cin zarafin yara. Kariya da kuma lura da cin zarafin yara na buƙatar wani tsararren yunƙurin duba zuwa ga dalilan da ke haddasa hatsarin fuskantar cin zarafi da hanyoyin kariya a dukkan matakan fuskantar barazanar guda huɗu masu alaƙa da juna ( ɗaiɗaikun mutane, mutane masu alaƙa da juna, unguwanni da al'umma). A Mayun 2016, yarjejeniyar Majalisar Lafiya ta Duniya ta amince da shirn Hukumar Lafiya ta duniya da za a aiwatar a faɗin Duniya a karo na farko wand azai ƙarfafi matsayin ɓangaren lafiya a wani yanki na ceto da ya ƙunshi ɓangarori da dama wajen magance cin zarafi tsakanin mutane makusanta, musamman kan manyan mata da ƙanana, da kuma yara. A bisa wannan shirin, Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya tare da haɗin gwiwar ƙasashen da ke ƙarƙashinta da sauran abokan hulɗa, sun jajirce wajen: Kula tsananin matsalar cin zarafin yara a duniya, da salon cin zarafin yara da kuma tallafawa ƙoƙarin ƙasa wajen tattarawa da auna irin wannan cin zarafin. Samar da tsarin samun bayanai ta na'ura wanda zai taƙaice bayanan kimiyya game da nauyi, dalilan shiga hatsari da sakamakon cin zarafin yara, da kuma shaidar yiwuwar kare aukuwar sa.
translations.reference: Violence against children Overview: Violence against children includes all forms of violence against people under 18 years old. For infants and younger children, violence mainly involves child maltreatment (i.e. physical, sexual and emotional abuse and neglect) at the hands of parents and other authority figures. Boys and girls are at equal risk of physical and emotional abuse and neglect, and girls are at greater risk of sexual abuse. As children reach adolescence, peer violence and intimate partner violence, in addition to child maltreatment, become highly prevalent. Violence against children can be prevented. Preventing and responding to violence against children requires that efforts systematically address risk and protective factors at all four interrelated levels of risk (individual, relationship, community, society). May 2016 World Health Assembly resolution endorsed the first ever WHO Global plan of action on strengthening the role of the health system within a national multisectoral response to address interpersonal violence, in particular against women and girls, and against children. A According to this plan, WHO in collaboration with Member States and other partners, is committed to: Monitoring the global magnitude and characteristics of violence against children and supporting country efforts to document and measure such violence. Maintaining an electronic information system that summarizes the scientific data on the burden, risk factors and consequences of violence against children, and the evidence for its preventability.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Hausa document into English.gs:

**Child Abuse: A Crucial Overview**

Child abuse encompasses any form of abuse inflicted upon individuals aged 18 and under.  Children and young people are particularly vulnerable to various forms of abuse, including physical abuse, sexual abuse, and neglect, often perpetrated by parents and other influential figures. Both boys and girls face the risk of sexual abuse, as well as psychological abuse and neglect, with girls being disproportionately affected by the risk of sexual abuse. As children mature, abuse between peers and close associates also increases significantly.  Preventing child abuse requires a targeted and comprehensive effort addressing the underlying causes of vulnerability and implementing protective measures at all levels – including caregivers, individuals in relationships, schools, and communities.

In May 2016, the World Health Assembly endorsed the resolution of the World Health Organization to implement a global program, strengthening the health sector as a key component of a response that includes multiple areas to address interpersonal violence, particularly among adult daughters and adult sons, and children.  Based on this program, the World Health Organization, in collaboration with its member states and other partners, is actively working to: address the global problem of child abuse, understand the patterns of child abuse, and support national efforts to document and assess such abuse.  Develop a data collection tool that will provide statistical data on the prevalence, risk factors, and consequences of child abuse, and to explore the possibility of preventing such occurrences.

translations.source: Samarwa da yaɗa takardun da ke nuna daburu, al'adu da matakan ba da kariya da ceto yara daga cin zarafi. Wallafa rahoton halin da ake ciki a duniya akai-akai game da ƙoƙarin ƙasa wajen magance cin zarafin yara ta hanyar manufofin ƙasa da tsare-tsaren ayyuka, dokoki, shirye-shiryen ba da kariya da ayyuka ceto. Ba da goyon baya ga ƙasashe da abokan hulɗa wajen aiwatar da dabarun kariya da ba da agaji kan lokaci da ke da shaidar aiki, kamar waɗanda ke cikin INSPIRE: Dabaru bakwai na kawo ƙarshen cin zarafin yara ƙanana. Akwai buƙatar haɗa kai da cibiyoyi da ƙungiyoyi na ƙasa da ƙasa wajen ragewa da kawo ƙarshen ɗabi’ar gallaza wa yara a faɗin duniya ta hanyar shirye-shirye kamar na Global Partnership to End Violence against Children, da Haɗakar ta Duniya don Yaƙi da Cutar da Yara, Ƙarfafar shirin mutane don bada kulawa kan afkuwar ibtila’i. Cin zarafin yara Cin zarafin yaro shi ne ɗabi’ar cuzgunawa ko nuna halin ko’in-kula da yara da ke ƙasa da shekaru 18 ke fuskanta. Da kan ƙunshi duk wani nau’i na cuzgunawa na fili ko na baɗili da haike wa yara da watsi da su da rashin nuna kulawa da saryar da su ko cin moriyarsu wanda hakan ƙarara ka iya zama cutarwa ga lafiyar yara, baraza ga rayuwarsu, cigabansu ko mutuncinsu a wani ɓangare da ya shafi watsi da alhakin kula da su ko amana. Ana kuma alamta gallazawa ta sako a wasu lokutan a matsayin nau’i na cin zarafin yara.
translations.reference: Developing and disseminating evidence-based technical guidance documents, norms and standards for preventing and responding to violence against children. Regularly publishing global status reports on country efforts to address violence against children through national policies and action plans, laws, prevention programmes and response services. Supporting countries and partners in implementing evidence-based prevention and response strategies, such as those included in INSPIRE: Seven strategies for ending violence against children. Collaborating with international agencies and organizations to reduce and eliminate violence against children globally, through initiatives such as the Global Partnership to End Violence against Children, the Global Partnership to End Violence against Children, Together for Girls and the Violence Prevention Alliance. Child maltreatment Child maltreatment is the abuse and neglect that occurs to children under 18 years of age. It includes all types of physical and/or emotional ill-treatment, sexual abuse, neglect, negligence and commercial or other exploitation, which results in actual or potential harm to the child’s health, survival, development or dignity in the context of a relationship of responsibility, trust or power. Exposure to intimate partner violence is also sometimes included as a form of child maltreatment.

“Collaboration to develop documents that demonstrate strategies, customs, and protective measures to rescue children from abuse.  The report presents a global, ongoing assessment of efforts made by countries to combat child abuse through national strategies, action plans, laws, protective programs, and rescue initiatives. It offers support to countries and partners in implementing protective and intervention strategies proven to be effective, such as those within the INSPIRE: Seven Principles to End Violence Against Children. There is a need for collaboration between national and international organizations to end the practice of child marriage globally through programs like the Global Partnership to End Violence Against Children and the United Nations Child Protection Compact.  Furthermore, support is needed for youth empowerment programs to address the impact of displacement. Child abuse encompasses any act of harm or neglect towards children under the age of 18. This includes any form of violence, whether direct or indirect, such as failing to provide care, affection, or education, or harming their health, safety, well-being, or life, even if it doesn't involve physical harm or direct violence.  Female genital mutilation is also recognized as a form of child abuse. ”

translations.source: Cin zarafin yara yana da tasiri mummuna na kusa ko nesa. Baya ga rasa rai da rauni ko tawaya, gallazawa na iya haifar da matsalar gazawar cigaban kwakwalwa ko ta jijiyoyi da ta garkuwar jiki baƙiɗaya. A sakamakon hakan, akan samu raunin hazaƙar kwakwalwa da rashin ƙoƙari a makaranta da rashin zuwa makarantar da larurorin kwakwalwa da yunƙurin kashe-kai, da samun ɗalibi’u da ka iya shafar lafiya da fargabar sake shiga mawuyacin hali da cigaba gallazawa. Labari mai daɗi shi ne, za a iya magance matsalar gallaza wa yara ta hanyoyin tallafa wa iyaye da masu kula da 'ya'ya da ke goyon bayan tsarin rayuwa da samar da ilimi da dabarun tafi da rayuwa da bunƙasa tattalin arziƙin iyali da tabbataccen abin yi da samar da tsarin kai ɗauki da ayyuka na taimako da tabbatar da ingantaccen muhalli ga yara da tabbatar da dokoki da suka shafi yaƙi da gallaza wa yara. Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya tare da haɗin guiwar wasu sa-hannu: suna wayar da kai wajen magance cin zarafin yara ta hanyar tabbatattun hujjoji.; Yin kira wajen samun tallafi a duniya bakiɗaya da kuma sa-hannu kan magance cin zarafin yara masu tabbatattun hujjoji.; Taimaka wa shirye-shirye na yaƙi da cin zarafin yara masu tabbatattun hujjoji a ƙasashe masu ƙarami da matsakaicin samu. gallazawa tsakanin matasa ko sako-da-sako Gallazawa tsakanin matasa matsala ce ta duniya da kan shafi lafiyar al'umma.
translations.reference: Child maltreatment has enormous immediate and long-term repercussions. Beyond death, physical injury and disability, violence can lead to stress that impairs brain development and damages the nervous and immune systems. This in turn is associated with delayed cognitive development, poor school performance and dropout, mental health problems, suicide attempts, increased health-risk behaviours, revictimization and the perpetration of violence. The good news is that child maltreatment can be prevented through interventions that support parents and caregivers, promote non-violent norms and values, provide education and life skills training, strengthen families’ income and economic security, offer high quality response and support services, create and sustain safe environments for children, and implement and enforce laws against child maltreatment. WHO, in collaboration with a number of partners: provides technical and normative guidance for evidence-based child maltreatment prevention; advocates for increased international support for and investment in evidence-based child maltreatment prevention; provides technical support for evidence-based child maltreatment prevention programmes in several low- and middle-income countries. Youth violence Youth violence is a global public health problem.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Hausa document into English.gs:

“Child abuse has a profound and lasting impact, whether nearby or far away. In addition to physical injury and trauma, violence can cause problems with the development of the reproductive system or internal organs. As a result, there can be damage to the reproductive organs and a lack of motivation for education and school attendance, as well as suicidal thoughts and attempts. It can also lead to individuals who negatively affect the health and fear of returning to a nurturing environment and continuing their development. Fortunately, the problem of child abuse can be addressed through supporting parents and caregivers who support a healthy lifestyle and education, as well as strategies for survival and economic development of families and stable employment.  Furthermore, providing access to assistance and ensuring a safe environment for children, and upholding laws related to combating child abuse is crucial. The World Health Organization, in collaboration with various organizations, is actively working to combat child abuse through documented evidence; advocating for international support and providing assistance to combat child abuse with documented evidence; and supporting programs to combat child abuse in small and medium-sized countries.  Youth violence or peer violence is a global issue that affects public health.”

**Notes on the Translation:**

*   I've aimed for a clear and accurate translation while preserving the original meaning.
*   Some Hausa phrases have been interpreted to convey the intended concept in English.
*   I've used more formal English to match the tone of the original document.

translations.source: Ta shafi abubuwa kamar na cuzgunawa da yin faɗa da haikewa da dukan kawo wuka da kisa-kai. Gallazawa tsakanin matasa na ƙara tazzara batun lafiya da walwala da shari'a kan manyan laifuka; hakan na rage himmar yin aiki da ma ɗaukacin martabar al'umma. Baya ga rasa rai da raunuka da shafar nutsuwa, gallazawa tsakanin matasa na iya haifar da ɗabi’un da kan illata lafiya kamar na shan taba da shan kayan maye da saduwa mai cutarwa da sauransu nau’ika na damuwa. A sakamakon hakan, a kan gamu da cutattukan numfashi masu tsanani da cutar daji da cutattuka da kan shafi zuciya da jijiyoyi da cutar siga da ɗaukar juna biyu a ƙananan shekaru da kamuwa da cutattuka da ke yaɗuwa ta saduwa. Hanya mafi tasiri wajen kariya da dabarun kai ɗauki sun haɗa da hanyoyin da suka ƙunshi ɗabbaƙa tsarin rainon ‘ya’ya da bunƙasa yara tun a farkon matakin yarinta.; Magance cuzgunawa a makarantu da na wuraren neman ilimi da ruyuwa da shirye-shirye na koyar da dabarun bunƙasa kai; Bin hanyoyin na waraka ga waɗanda halin da suke ciki ya ta’azzara da bin tsari na magance matsalar a mataki na al’umma kmar rage yawan damar samu da wuce gona-da-iri wajen ta’ammali da giya, da bindigu da wuraren masu sa fargaba da amfani da tsarin nemo mafita ga matsaloli da bunƙasa birane da daƙile bazuwar talauci. Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya da sauran abokan haɗin guiwa sun yi nasarar rage gallazawa tsakanin matasa ta hanyar shirye-shirye da suka taimaka wajen ganowa da gano adadi da ɗaukar mataki kan matsalar da suka haɗa: Samar da tallafi ga shirye-shirye na kawo ƙarshen gallazawa da ke faruwa a makarantu ta hanyar; Kururuta girman gallazawa tsakanin matasa da kuma buƙatar kawo ƙarshen matsalar.;
translations.reference: It includes a range of acts from bullying and physical fighting, to more severe sexual and physical assault to homicide. Youth violence dramatically increases health, welfare and criminal justice costs; reduces productivity; and generally undermines the fabric of society. Beyond deaths, injuries and psychological harm, youth violence can lead to increased health risk behaviours such as smoking, substance abuse, unsafe sex, and further violence. These in turn are associated with chronic respiratory diseases, cancer, cardiovascular diseases, diabetes, early pregnancy and sexually transmitted diseases. Effective prevention and response strategies include those that promote parenting and early childhood development; school-based bullying prevention, academic, and life and social skills development programmes; therapeutic approaches with high-risk individuals, and community- and society-level approaches like reducing access to and misuse of alcohol and firearms, hotspots and problem-oriented policing, urban upgrading, and poverty de-concentration. WHO and partners decrease youth violence through initiatives that help to identify, quantify and respond to the problem, these include: developing a package for schools-based violence prevention programmes; drawing attention to the magnitude of youth violence and the need for prevention;
translations.hypothesis:

The document addresses issues such as teenage pregnancy, sexual violence, social isolation, and suicide. Young people are increasingly discussing health, well-being, and justice regarding major problems; this reduces the incidence of work and significantly increases community engagement. In addition to losing hope and experiencing emotional distress, peer pressure can lead to harmful behaviors such as drug use, unsafe sexual practices, and others that contribute to anxiety. As a result, there is an increase in serious mental health conditions, cancer, heart and circulatory diseases, and neurological disorders, as well as childhood injuries and the spread of infectious diseases through sexual contact. The most effective ways to prevent this include interventions that involve child protection systems and child development programs from an early age. Addressing teenage pregnancy in schools and educational institutions through awareness campaigns and training on positive parenting strategies is crucial. Promoting healthy relationships for those struggling with addiction and following a clear path to address the problem at the community level, while reducing the risk of exposure to harmful substances like alcohol, drugs, and places that facilitate abuse, and utilizing problem-solving strategies for youth development is important. The Global Health Organization and other partners have successfully reduced teenage relationships through programs that identify and respond to the issue by providing support for programs that end teenage relationships in schools through; strengthening teenage relationships and addressing the problem.

Haɗa kai da hukumomi da ƙungiyoyin ƙasashe wajen magance gallazawa tsakanin matasa a duniya gabaɗaya.

translations.source: Cin zarafin mata A dunƙule: Cin zarafin mata - musamman cin zarafi daga abokin zama da kuma cin zarafi ta fuskar saduwa - ɗaya ne daga cikin ƙalubale da suke shafar harkar lafiya da kuma keta ‘yancin bil’Adama da ya danganci haƙƙin mata. Tushensa da faruwar hakan na da nasaba da rashin daidaito na jinsi. A duniya bakiɗaya, 1 cikin 3 na mata sukan fuskanci cin zarafi ko haikewa a rayuwarsu, musammman daga mafi kusanci da su. Wannan muhimmin tunatarwa ne game da irin girman rashin daidaito na jinsi da nuna bambanci ga mata. Majalisar Ɗinkin Duniya ta bayyana cin zarafin mata da cewa; “duk wani abu da za aikata da ya shafi kallon jinsi a siga ta cin zarafi da ke haifar ko ka iya kai wa ga shafar mutum a fili, ko ta saduwa ko sosa rai ko shiga mawuyacin halli da kuma ya haɗa har da barazanar aikata waɗannan abubuwa ko tilastawa ko tauye ‘yancin walwala ko a bayyanar jama’a ko a keɓe. Cin zarafi daga abokin zama wata ɗabi’a ce da abokin zama ko wanda aka rabu ke yi da ka iya shafar nutsuwa ta zahiri ko ta baɗini ko ta saduwa da kan ƙunshi yin faɗa a fili ko tilasta saduwa ko haifar da fargaba da bibiyar rayuwar mutum. Cin zarafi na jima’i “wani yunƙuri ne na biyan buƙata ko bin wasu hanyoyi da suka danganci haka ta hanyar amfani da ƙarfin tuwo daga kowanne mutum ko mai kuwa kusancinsa da wanda abin ya shafa a kowanne irin yanayi. Wannan ya haɗa da: fyaɗe wanda aka ayyana a matsayin amfani da ƙarfi a fili wajen shiga budurci ta gaba ko ta baya ta amfani da azzakari ko wani abu daban. Tasiri: Cin zarafin mata matsala ce a duniya bakiɗaya da ya zama ruwan dare. Yana haifar da mummanan illa ga rayuwar mata da ta ‘ya’yansu.
translations.reference: Violence against women Overview: Violence against women – particularly intimate partner violence and sexual violence – is a major public and clinical health problem and a violation of women's human rights. It is rooted in and perpetuates gender inequalities.Globally 1 in 3 women experience physical and/or sexual violence in their lifetime, mostly by an intimate partner. This is a stark reminder of the scale of gender inequality and discrimination against women. The United Nations defines violence against women as "any act of gender-based violence that results in, or is likely to result in, physical, sexual, or mental harm or suffering to women, including threats of such acts, coercion or arbitrary deprivation of liberty, whether occurring in public or in private life. Intimate partner violence refers to behaviour by an intimate partner or ex-partner that causes physical, sexual or psychological harm, including physical aggression, sexual coercion, psychological abuse and controlling behaviours. Sexual violence is "any sexual act, attempt to obtain a sexual act, or other act directed against a person’s sexuality using coercion, by any person regardless of their relationship to the victim, in any setting. It includes rape, defined as the physically forced or otherwise coerced penetration of the vulva or anus with a penis, other body part or object." Impact: Violence against women is a global problem of pandemic proportions. It causes devastating, harm to women’s lives and that of their children.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Hausa document into English.gs:

**Violence Against Women and Child Abuse: Violence against women – particularly sexual violence from partners and also sexual violence through coercion – is one of the challenges that affect the health sector and violate human rights, which is closely related to gender inequality. Globally, 1 in 3 women experience sexual violence or coercion in their lives, especially from those closest to them. This is a significant reminder of the extent of gender inequality and the discrimination against women. The United Nations defines violence against women as: “any act of violence that is perpetrated because of gender, whether through physical, sexual, or psychological abuse, including coercion, degradation, or violence that affects the health, safety, dignity, or fulfillment of women.”  Sexual violence is a form of demand or compliance through means related to it, using power dynamics from one person to another in any situation. This includes: forcing a girl into sexual activity, either through pressure, threats, or other means. Consequences: Violence against women is a global problem that has become a nightmare. It has devastating effects on the lives of women and their children.**

---

translations.source: Haka kuma yana shafar tattalin arziƙi da lafiyar iyali da al’umma da ma ƙasashen da ake samun hakan. Kodayake da wasu matan sun fi wasu fuskantar wannan barazana, sai dai cin zarafi abu ne da kowacce mace na iya fuskanta a kowacce ƙasa ba tare da la’akari da al’ada ko addini ko yanayin tattalin arziƙi ba. Rashin daidaito na jinsi da wasu al’adu game da jinsi su ne musabbabin cin zarafin mata. Cin zarafi kan iya illata mata a zahiri ko a baɗili ko fuskar saduwa ko lafiyar mahaifarsu. Ana alaƙanta hakan da ƙaruwar ciwuka na fili da cutar damuwa da larura ta yawan fargaba da samun juna biyu da kamuwa da cututtuka masu yaɗuwa da HIV da sauran matsaloli na lafiya. Yaran da suka taso a gidajen da ake fuskantar cin zarafi kan gamu da matsaloli da kan shafi ɗabi’arsu. Wannan kan shafi tsarin bunƙasa tunani da kuma ke tasiri wajen fuskanta ko aikata cin zarafin a rayuwar mutum. Matakin Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya: Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya ta taka muhimmiyar rawa wajen jan hankali ga abin da ya shafi cin zarafin mata a matsayin lamari da ya shafi lafiya da rashin daidaito na jinsi ta hanyar: Bincike da tattara hujjoji wajen nuna girman matsalar cin zarafin mata da illolinta da sakamakonta da kuma zaƙulo sahihan hanyoyin kai ɗauki domin hana afkuwar hakan da tallafawa. Samar da tsari na cigaba da ƙirƙirar dokoki da cimma matsaya ingantacciya wajen duba lafiyar matan da suka fuskanci cin zarafi.
translations.reference: It also hurts the economic and social health of their families, communities, and countries. While some women are more at risk than others, violence can happen to any woman, in any country – regardless of culture, religion or economic status. Gender inequality including harmful gender norms are key drivers of violence against women. Violence can negatively affect women’s physical, mental, sexual, and reproductive health. It is associated with increased risk of injuries, depression, anxiety disorders, unplanned pregnancies, sexually transmitted infections, HIV and many other health problems. Children who grow up in families where there is violence may suffer a range of behavioural and emotional disturbances. These can lead to mental and development problems and also be associated with perpetrating or experiencing violence later in life. WHO response: WHO plays a key role in bringing attention to and responding to violenceagainst women as a public health and gender equality issue through: Research and evidence-building to highlight the magnitude of violence against women, its risk factors and consequences, and to identify effective interventions for prevention and response. Developing guidelines and tools, setting norms and standards for an effective health response to violence against women.

Gender inequality and certain practices related to gender are the causes of women’s rape. Rape can harm women physically, psychologically, or by damaging their reproductive health or the health of their mothers. This is linked to increased rates of sexually transmitted infections, depression, domestic violence, exposure to multiple partners, the spread of HIV, and other health issues. Young people who have experienced sexual violence can suffer from psychological problems and difficulties in their development. This can affect the way they think and have an impact on their experience of or involvement in perpetrating sexual violence in their lives.

translations.source: Gobarar daji Taƙaitaccen bayani: yawan ƙaruwar afkuwar gobarar daji abu ne da ke nuni da buƙatar fahimtar ƙalubale na lafiya da wutar daji ke da shi ga waɗanda ya ritsa da su. Illar wutar daji na ƙaruwa matuƙa a wuri da ke ƙamfar ruwa kamar inda ake da ƙarancin ruwan sama da tsananin zafi da iska mai ƙarfi. Hayaƙin wutar daji na ɗauke da sinadai masu guba da ke gurɓata iska kamar PM2.5 da ozone da hydrocarbon ko lead. Sannan, game da batun gurɓata iska da sinadarai masu guba, gobarar daji a lokaci guda na shafar sauyin yanayi ta hanyar fitar da iska ga sararin samaniya a matsayin hayaƙin da ke ɗauke da kabon-dai-oksida da sauran nau'ikan iska da ke cuɗanya da zafi. La'akari da sauyin yanayi da ke kan gaba wajen ɗumama yanayi da yanayi bushewar wuri da ƙaruwar ɗabi'ar mai da ƙauyuka birane, akwai yuwuwar gobarar na iya fara ruruwa da wuri da kuma ɗaukar lokaci mai ɗan tsawo. afkuwar gobarar daji na cikgaba da tsananta ta hanyar ƙone kadada mai yawa da irin lokaci da ƙarfin da gobarar ke da shi wanda tana iya kawo cikas ga harkokin sufuri da sadarwa da samar da ruwan sha da samar da makamashi da iskar gas. Tasiri: gobarar daji da ke ƙone wurare masu cukowar al'umma zai iya shafar muhalli da dukiya da dabbobi da ɗabi'u da kamuwa da cutattuka duk da ya danganta da girman wurin da sauri da kusanci da gobarar da kuma yuwuwar samun gargaɗi na barin wurin da abin ka iya faruwa. hayaƙin gobarar daji haɗe yake da batun gurɓata iska wanda ya sanya ta ɗaya daga cikin lura a fannin lafiyar al'umma. ana alaƙanta mutuwar fuju'a ta mutane da sinadarai masu girman PM2.5 da ake samu a cikin iskar gobarar daji wanda kuma ke taɓarɓara cutattukan da suka shafi hunhu da na zuciya da tsarin zubin ƙwaƙwalwa da jijiyoyi da fata da hanji da ƙoda da idanu da hanci da hanta. sannan bincike ya tabbatar da cewa takan iya shafar sashen tunanin abin da aka saba yi na ƙwaƙwalwa da mantuwa.
translations.reference: Wildfires Overview: Wildfires are increasing around the globe in frequency, severity and duration, heightening the need to understand the health effects of wildfire exposure. The risk of wildfires grows in extremely dry conditions, such as drought, heat waves and during high winds.Wildfire smoke is a mixture of hazardous air pollutants, such PM2.5, NO2, ozone, aromatic hydrocarbons, or lead. In addition to contaminating the air with toxic pollutants, wildfires also simultaneously impact the climate by releasing large quantities of carbon dioxide and other greenhouse gases into the atmosphere. With climate change leading to warmer temperatures and drier conditions and the increasing urbanization of rural areas, the fire season is starting earlier and ending later. Wildfire events are getting more extreme in terms of acres burned, duration and intensity, and they can disrupt transportation, communications, water supply, and power and gas services. Impact: Wildfires that burn near populated areas can have significant impact on the environment, property, livestock and human mortality and morbidity depending on the size, speed and proximity to the fire, and whether the population has advanced warning to evacuate. Wildfire smoke is a mixture of air pollutants of which particulate matter (PM) is the principal public health threat. PM2.5 from wildfire smoke is associated with premature deaths in the general population, and can cause and exacerbate diseases of the lungs, heart, brain/nervous system, skin, gut, kidney, eyes, nose and liver. It has also been shown to lead to cognitive impairment and memory loss.

**Dust Storm Data:  The increase in dust storms is an indicator of the health challenges and the impact of energy use on those who experience them.  Dust storm intensity is particularly high in areas near water bodies, characterized by low rainfall, high temperatures, and strong winds.**

**Dust storms capture pollutants such as particulate matter (PM2.5), ozone, hydrocarbons, and lead. Furthermore, regarding air pollution and pollutants, dust storms simultaneously affect climate change by releasing particles into the atmosphere, acting as a carrier of carbon dioxide and other atmospheric gases that contribute to warming.  Considering the ongoing climate change leading to altered weather patterns, desertification, and urbanization, there is a possibility that dust storms will intensify and shorten their duration.**

**The fallout from dust storms continues to expand through numerous connections and durations, depending on the size and strength of the storm, which can negatively impact transportation, communication, water supply, energy production, and gas distribution.  Impact: Dust storms in densely populated areas can affect the environment, livelihoods, animals, and ecosystems, and can contribute to the spread of diseases. The proximity of the dust storm and the potential for early warning are also factors.**

**Dust storm emissions are linked to air pollution, making it a key concern in public health.  Studies have shown a correlation between mortality rates in humans and high levels of PM2.5 in dust storm emissions, which can trigger respiratory illnesses such as asthma, heart disease, lung cancer, and reproductive problems, including infertility, birth defects, and eye, ear, nose, throat, and stomach ailments.**

**Research also indicates that dust storms can affect mental health, potentially leading to anxiety and a desire to avoid affected areas.**

---

**Notes on the Translation:**

*   **Context:** I've tried to maintain the context of the document, which discusses the environmental and health impacts of dust storms.
*   **Flow:** I've aimed for a natural and readable flow in English.

translations.reference: Firefighters and emergency response workers are also greatly impacted by injuries, burns and smoke inhalation, particularly at high concentrations. More interdisciplinary research is warranted to understand the latent and long-term health effects of wildfire exposure on vulnerable populations (children, older people, pregnant people, chronically ill people), particularly for geographic areas enduring repeated and cyclical exposure to these wildﬁre events. WHO Response: The magnitude of the physical and human costs from wildfires can be reduced if adequate emergency prevention, preparedness, response and recovery measures are implemented in a sustainable and timely manner. Working with Member States, WHO helps to build resilient and proactive health systems that can anticipate the needs and challenges during emergencies, so that we can be more likely to reduce risks and respond effectively when needed. WHO works with partners in preparing, preventing, detecting, responding and recovering from emergencies and disasters, including environmental exposures, such asimplementing early warning systems and issuing health and air quality advisories; containing the release of hazardous materials; assessing health needs of the community and infrastructure damage; restoring primary care services, like immunization, child and maternal health, and mental health; strengthening human resources for disaster management; collecting, analysing and disseminating information related to emergencies and disasters that are likely to occur in a region; establishing and managing stocks of relief supplies and equipment;

“Those affected by uranium mining and its associated risks include a wide range of individuals. Diverse research areas indicate the long-term impact of uranium mining on the health of those affected, including children, adults, and those with pre-existing conditions, particularly in areas with frequent uranium mine accidents.

The World Health Organization’s (WHO) position: The impact of uranium on the community can be reduced if effective methods for addressing it quickly and sustainably are developed, along with consistent monitoring and testing. Collaboration with WHO member states to establish robust health systems that can cope with emergencies and potential disruptions, ensuring timely and appropriate testing, is crucial.

The WHO will work with community health workers to identify, address, and mitigate the consequences of emergencies and disasters, as well as raise public awareness about the risks of uranium exposure through timely warnings.  This includes providing information on hazardous materials and assessing the community’s health and resource needs.

Prioritizing primary healthcare, such as vaccination and improving maternal, child, and animal health, and community health monitoring is essential in combating the disease.  Raising public awareness about the dangers of radiation exposure is also vital.

Furthermore, it is necessary to receive and disseminate information on potential emergencies and disasters that are anticipated to occur in a specific area; to establish and maintain support systems and other supplies.”

translations.source: Kiwon lafiyar mata Taƙaitaccen bayani: kasancewa namiji ko mace abu ne mai muhimmanci ga batun lafiya saboda irin bambance-bambance da ke samu na halitta. Lafiyar mata da ƙananan yara mata na da muhimmanci domin a wasu al'ummatan sukan rasa gata saboda bambanci da ke da alaƙa da al'ada ta rayuwar yau da kullum. Ga misali mata da ƙananan yara mata na fuskantar barazanar kamuwa da HIV/AIDS. wasu daga ciki dalilai masu alaƙa da al'ada ta yau da kullum da ke daƙile mata daga samun ingantaccen kulawa ta lafiya sun hada da: bambancin iko da ke akwai tsakanin maza da mata; Tsarin zamantakewa da ke daƙile ilimi da batun damarmaki na aikin da za a biya mutum; Ba wa ɓangaren batun rawar mata wajen haihuwa muhimmanci; da da kuma fuskantar cin zarafi na sarari ko na fannin saduwa ko na imoshan. Duk da talauci na taka rawa wajen kawo cikas ga samun ingantaccen kiwon lafiya ga maza da mata; mata da ƙananan yara mata sun fi fuskantar ƙalubalen talauci, ga misali ɓangaren cin abinci (rashin wadataccen abinci) da amfani da abin girki masu illa (COPD).
translations.reference: Women's health Overview: Being a man or a woman has a significant impact on health, as a result of both biological and gender-related differences. The health of women and girls is of particular concern because, in many societies, they are disadvantaged by discrimination rooted in sociocultural factors. For example, women and girls face increased vulnerability to HIV/AIDS. Some of the sociocultural factors that prevent women and girls to benefit from quality health services and attaining the best possible level of health include: unequal power relationships between men and women; social norms that decrease education and paid employment opportunities; an exclusive focus on women’s reproductive roles; and potential or actual experience of physical, sexual and emotional violence. While poverty is an important barrier to positive health outcomes for both men and women, poverty tends to yield a higher burden on women and girls’ health due to, for example, feeding practices (malnutrition) and use of unsafe cooking fuels (COPD).

**Women's Health: A Concise Report: Determining whether a person is male or female is important in the context of health due to the inherent differences that exist. Maternal and child health is crucial, as some communities experience female genital mutilation (FGM) due to cultural practices. Women and children face the risk of contracting HIV/AIDS, with some factors related to daily life and limited access to quality healthcare including: gender inequality between men and women; social structures that limit education and the right to paid work; insufficient attention given to the role of women in childbirth; and the risk of sexual, domestic, or coercive violence. Despite the limited role in accessing quality healthcare for both men and women, women and children are more likely to face challenges, such as food insecurity (lack of adequate nutrition) and exposure to harmful household practices (COPD).**

---

translations.source: cutar fata (Endemic treponematoses) Taƙaitaccen bayani: cutar fata ta Yaws cutar fata ce mai tsananin gaske da ke da yanayi na kumburi da gyambo (ciwo). Bakteriyar da ake kira Treponema Pallidum ce ke haifar da wannan cutar fata wacce aka sanya ta cikin kashin baktariyar da ke haifar da sifilis da ake samu ta hanyar saduwa. cutar fata ta Yaws ta fi shafar yara 'yan ƙasa da shekaru 15 da ke rayuwa cikin talauci a wuri mai yanayin zafi a yankin Afirka da Asiya da Latin America da tsibirin Fasifik. Mafi yawan waɗanda ke kamuwa da cutar na rayuwa ne a ƙauyuka da ke da nisa da wuraren kiwon lafiya. Talauci da yanayi na rashin abin hannu da rashin tsabta ne ke taimakawa wajen yaɗuwar cutar fata ta Yaws. duk da akwai rahotanni 80000 na kamuwa da cutar fata ta Yaws a duk shekara, masana sun yi amanna cewa za a iya magance cutar da ma kawar da ita saboda da dalilai masu yawa. Da farko dai ana samun cutar ne a cikin bil'Adama ba dabba ba wanda ke nuna sauƙin magance cutar. Za a iya magance cutar cikin sauƙi ta hanyar amfani da magunguna da tuni akwai su kamar yadda aka yi nasarar kawar da cutar a wasu ƙasashe kamar Indiya. Wasu 'yan ƙalilan da ke da cutar na zaune a keɓantattun wurare wanda hakan ke nuna sauƙin daƙile yaɗuwarta idana aka yi amfani da matakai na bibiya da kulawa.
translations.reference: Yaws (Endemic treponematoses) Overview: Yaws is a chronic skin infection characterized by papillomas (noncancerous lumps) and ulcers. It is caused by the bacterium Treponema pallidum subspecies pertenue, which belongs to the same group of bacteria that causes venereal syphilis. Yaws primarily affects children aged under 15 years who live in poor communities in warm, humid and tropical forested areas of Africa, Asia, Latin America and the Pacific islands. The majority of affected populations live rural areas, far from health services. Poverty, low socio-economic conditions and poor personal hygiene facilitate the spread of yaws. Although there are over 80 000 cases of yaws each year, experts believe the disease can be controlled and ultimately eradicated for several reasons. First, it only occurs in humans, not animals, making control much easier. It is also easy to treat with readily available drugs and has already been eliminated in some countries, including India. The remaining pockets of yaws infection, although usually in remote places, means further spread is less likely with proper surveillance and control measures.

**Yaws (Endemic Treponematoses):**

**Background Information:** Yaws is a serious, persistent skin disease characterized by sores (lesions). The bacterium *Treponema pallidum* causes this skin disease, the same bacterium that causes syphilis and is transmitted through sexual contact.

Yaws primarily affects young people under the age of 15 living in poverty in Africa, Asia, and Latin America and the Pacific Islands. The highest incidence of the disease is found in rural communities far from healthcare facilities. Poverty and unsanitary conditions contribute to the spread of Yaws. Despite reports of 80,000 cases of Yaws each year, experts believe the disease can be treated and eventually eradicated due to numerous reasons.

Initially, the infection is found within bone tissue, which makes treatment more challenging. However, treatment can be relatively simple using antibiotics that are currently available.  Success has been achieved in eradicating the disease in countries like India. A small number of people with the disease live in isolated areas, which indicates a greater difficulty in its spread and therefore requires more targeted intervention strategies.

---

**Notes on the Translation:**

*   I've used "Endemic Treponematoses" as the standard English term for "cutar fata (Endemic treponematoses)."
*   I've tried to maintain the tone and information presented in the original Hausa text.
*   I've included explanations of terms like "lesions" to aid understanding for an English-speaking audience.

translations.source: Alamomin kamuwa: Akwai matakai biyu na kwamuwa da cutar fata ta Yaws. mataki na farko shi ne matakin da mai larurar ke ɗauke da cutar kuma zai iya yaɗawa ga sauran mutane. a wannan mataki a kan samu ɓullowar ƙurji (wanda bai shafi kansa ba da ke ɓullowa kamar ƙurji) wanda ke fara girma da zarar mutum ya kamu da shi. ƙurjin yana dauke da ƙwayar cutar da kuma kan ɗauki watanni 3-6 ana maganinsa. Idan kuma ba a ɗauki mataki na magani ba, yana cigaba da yaɗuwa ne a kan fatar zuwa sauran sassan jiki. ana iya fara jin raɗaɗi daga cikin ƙashi da musayar lafiyayyen ƙashi da wani. mataki na biyu shi ne matakin da cutar ba za ta iya yaɗuwa ba wanda hakan na faruwa ne kusan shekaru biyar da kamuwa da cutar. ana iya fayyace cutar da ƙandarewar hanci da ƙashi da tafin hannu/dunduniya (kaurara). bayan nazarin alamomin cutar, za a iya tabbatar da cutar ne kawai idan aka haska fatar da wata nau'in nau'irar ganin ƙwaƙwaf ta musamman (darkfield examination). ba cika amfani da gwajin jini ba saboda cutar fata ta yaws na da alaƙa da bakteriyar da ke haifar da cutar sifilis, don haka sakamako ɗaya za a samu na cututtukan biyu.
translations.reference: Symptoms: There are two stages of yaws infection. The early stage is when the patient is infectious and can spread the disease to others. In this stage, a papule (a noncancerous, outward-growing lump) develops at the site of infection. This papule is full of the organisms and may persist for 3–6 months followed by natural healing. Without treatment, this is followed by disseminated skin lesions over the body. Bone pain and bone lesions may also occur. The second stage is noninfectious and typically appears five years after infection. It is characterized by disabling consequences of the nose, bones and palmar/plantar hyperkeratosis (thickening). After a field diagnosis based on these symptoms, the disease can be confirmed by examining a sample from a skin legion under a special type of microscope (darkfield examination). Blood tests are not typically used because yaws is closely related to the bacterium that causes syphilis and the two diseases will show the same result.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Hausa document into English.gs:

**Subject: There are two stages to Yaws (cutaneous) disease. The first stage is the stage where the infected person has the disease and can transmit it to others. At this stage, a dark ulcer (which is not self-limiting) will begin to grow as the person becomes infected. The ulcer contains the bacteria causing the disease and can take 3-6 months to heal. If treatment is not taken, it continues to spread throughout the skin. Symptoms may begin in one area of the skin and spread to other areas. The second stage is the stage where the disease can no longer spread, which occurs approximately five years after infection. The disease can be detected with a darkfield microscope and examination of the skin and nails/fingers/toes (fungi). After examining the symptoms, the disease can be confirmed only if a special type of darkfield examination of the skin is performed (darkfield examination). Blood tests are not used because Yaws disease is related to bacteria that cause syphilis, so a single test will usually yield results for both diseases.**

---

**Notes on the translation:**

*   I've aimed for clarity and accuracy while maintaining a natural English style.
*   "Alamomin kamuwa" translates to "Symptoms of infection/disease."
*   "ɓullowar ƙurji" is translated as "dark ulcer."
*   "Ƙwayar cutar" is translated as "bacteria causing the disease."

translations.source: Magani: A yanzu ba babu wata rigakafi ta cutar fata ta yaws. Ilimantarwa da inganta tsabtar jiki su ne muhimman dabarun da ake amfani da su na kariya. ana iya amfani da nau'o'in antibayotik guda biyu wajen maganin cutar fata ta yaws. an yi ba da shawarar amfani da maganin sha na azithromycin (30 mg/kg, maximum 2 g). za a iya amfani da maganin Benzathine penicillin (ta hanyar allura sau ɗaya) a kan waon 0.6 (ga yara 'yan ƙasa da shekaru 10) da awon 1.2 (ga yara 'yan sama da shekaru 10) ga marasa lafiya da ake zargin gazawar maganin da aka ɗora su a kai bayan azithromycin ko marasa lafiyar da laurarsu ta fi ƙarfin azithromycin. wannan mataki na magani sai warkar kashi 95% na masu cutar, sai dai akwai buƙatar sake duba su bayan mako 4 da karɓar antibiyotik ɗin. wani bincike ya nuna bayanan da suka gabata daga 1950 cewa ƙasashe 90 da yankuna suna fama da cutar fata ta yaws. kuma 14 cikin 90 na ƙasashen ne ke da bayanan na kusa-kusa a kan cutar fata ta yaws idan aka yi la'akari da tsarin bibiyar cutar, duk da cewa waɗannan al'ƙaluma ka iya zama manuniya ce ta wanzuwar wannan cutar, sai dai an rage mata ƙarfi. Ƙasashen Ghana da Papua New Guineada Tsibirin Solomon sun tabbatar da cewa suna samun sama masu cutar 10000 a kowacce shekara. Ƙasashen Ecuado da India sun yi nasarar daƙile yaɗuwar cutar.
translations.reference: Treatment: There is currently no vaccine for yaws. Health education and improved personal hygiene are the most important tactics for prevention. Two antibiotics can be used to treat yaws. A single oral dose of azithromycin (30 mg/kg, maximum 2 g) is the recommended treatment. Benzathine penicillin (single intramuscular dose) at 0.6 million units (children aged under 10 years) and 1.2 million units (people aged over 10 years) can be used for patients with suspected clinical treatment failure after azithromycin, or patients who cannot be treated with azithromycin. This treatment will result in complete clinical healing in 95% of cases but patients should be re-examined 4 weeks after receiving the antibiotics. A review of the historic documents from 1950s shows that over 90 countries and territories were endemic for yaws. Only 14 out of the 90 countries and territories have recent data on yaws based on the routine surveillance system; however, these figures may just be an indication of the presence of the disease and not its full extent. Ghana, Papua New Guinea and the Solomon Islands report over 10 000 cases per year. Ecuador and India appear to have interrupted transmission.

Two types of antibiotics can be used to treat malaria. It is recommended to use azithromycin (30 mg/kg, maximum 2 g) orally. Countries like Ghana, Papua New Guinea, and the Solomon Islands have confirmed cases of around 10,000 per year. Countries like Ecuador and India have successfully reduced the spread of malaria.**

translations.source: Zazzaɓin masassara ko shawara Taƙaitaccen bayani: zazzaɓin masassara ko shawara cuta ce da 'yan Adam ke kamuwa da ita ta hanayr ciwon sauri. akwai yuwuwar yaɗuwarsa kuma ana iya magance ta ta hanyar rigakafi. waɗannan sauraye da ke cizo na iya hayayyafa a kusa da muhallan mutane (domestic) da dazuka (wild) da kuma duka muhallan biyu (semi-domestic). masassara ko shawara zazzaɓi ne mai tasiri kuma cuta mai illar gaske da ke da yuwuwar yaɗuwa a duniya wanda ake kallo a matsayin barazana ga kiwon lafiya a duniya. akwai hanyoyi 3 na hanyoyin yaɗuwar cutar: Sylvatic; cutar na yaɗuwa ne ta hanyar ciwon da sauraye ke yi wa birrai wanda daga nan ne suke yaɗa wa sauran birrai da mutane. zazzaɓin masassara ko shawara tsakanin halittu biyu na yaɗuwa ne a lokacin da sauraye suka harbi birrai da mutane. wannan nau'i ne na cuta ake yawan samun ɓarkewarsa a yankin Afirka. Zazzaɓin masassara na iya yaɗuwa a birane idan mutanen da suka harbu tare da yaɗa ta a wuraren da ke da yawan sauraye a kuma muhallin da mutane ke da ƙarancin kariya daga cutar. a wasu lokutan matafiya ne yaɗa wannan zazzaɓi na masassara a ƙasashen da ba su da ita.
translations.reference: Yellow fever Overview: Yellow fever is a viral disease that is transmitted to humans by the bites of infected mosquitoes. It is prone to epidemics and is preventable with a vaccine. These day-biting mosquitoes breed around houses (domestic), in forests or jungles (wild), or in both habitats (semi-domestic). Yellow fever is a high-impact, high-threat disease, with a risk of international spread, representing a potential threat to global health security. There are 3 types of transmission cycles: Sylvatic (or jungle) yellow fever spreads when monkeys are bitten by wild mosquitoes that pass the virus on to other monkeys and humans. Intermediate yellow fever spreads when semi-domestic mosquitoes infect both monkeys and people. This is the most common type of outbreak in Africa. Urban yellow fever can cause large epidemics when infected people introduce the virus into heavily populated areas with high mosquito density and where people have little immunity. Occasionally, infected travellers have exported cases to countries that are free of yellow fever.

**Zizzy Flies: A Brief Overview**

Zizzy flies, or sandflies, are a disease that humans contract rapidly. There is a possibility of its spread, and it can be managed through medication. These flies can live near human environments (domestic) and wild environments, and both types of environments.

Zizzy flies are a significant disease and a serious illness with a potential for global spread, which is viewed as a threat to global health. There are three ways for the disease to spread: Sylvatic; the disease spreads through flies biting animals, which then transmit it to other animals and humans. Zizzy flies can also spread when flies bite both animals and humans. This type of disease is commonly found in Africa.

Zizzy flies can spread to urban areas if people come into contact with flies carrying the disease in areas with a high concentration of flies and where people are vulnerable to the disease. In some cases, travelers have spread this zizzy fly in countries where it is not found.

Alamomin kamuwa da cutar: zazzaɓin masassara na da alamomin mabambanta. da zarar mutum ya kamu, ƙwayar cutar na ƙyanƙyashewa a jikinsa ne tsakanin kwanaki 3-6. Wasu mutane ba za sa jin alamun kamuwa, amma idan hakan ta faru, mafi yawan alamun da mutum ke ji shi ne zazzaɓi, zugi tare da ciwon baya da ciwon kai da rashin son cin abinci da jin hararwa ko yin amai. A mafi yawancin lokuta akan nemi alamun zazzaɓin a rasa bayan kwanaki 3-4. wasu daga cikin marasa lafiya suna shiga mataki na biyu kuma mafi haɗari bayan awanni 24 daga daina jin alamomin farko. Zazzaɓi mai zafi na dawowa da kuma ke shafar sassan jiki da dama kamar hanta da ƙodoji. A wannan mataki, akwai yuwuwar mutane su fuskanci sauyin launi a wani sashe na jiki (sauyawar launin fata da ƙwayar idanu zuwa launin rawaya wanda hakan ya sa ake kiran cutar da 'yellow fever') da bawali mai launin baƙi da ciwon ciki da hararwa. Ana iya samun zubar jini daga baki da hanci da idanu ko ciki. Rabin waɗannan marasa lafiya sun mutu a cikin kwananki 7-10.cutar zazzaɓin yana da wuyar ganewa,musamman a lokacin farkon matakan.
translations.reference: However, the disease can only spread easily to a new country if there are mosquito species able to transmit it, specific climatic conditions, and the animal reservoir needed to maintain it. Symptoms: Yellow fever can present with a wide range of symptoms and severity. Once contracted, the yellow fever virus incubates in the body for 3–6 days. Many people do not experience symptoms, but when these do occur, the most common are fever, muscle pain with prominent backache, headache, loss of appetite, and nausea or vomiting. In most cases, symptoms disappear after 3–4 days. A small percentage of patients enter a second, more toxic phase within 24 hours of recovering from initial symptoms. High fever returns and several body systems are affected, usually the liver and kidneys. In this phase, people are likely to develop jaundice (yellowing of the skin and eyes, hence the name yellow fever), dark urine, and abdominal pain with vomiting. Bleeding can occur from the mouth, nose, eyes, or stomach. Half of these patients die within 7–10 days.Yellow fever is difficult to diagnose, especially during the early stages.

translations.source: Za'a iya rikitar da lamura masu tsanani tare da matsanancin zazzabin cizon sauro, leptospirosis, hepatitis na kwayar cuta (musamman nau'ikan fulminant), sauran zazzabi na haemorrhagic, kamuwa da wasu flaviviruses (kamar zazzabin haemorrhagic na dengue) da guba. Gwajin polymerase{PCR} a cikin jini da fitsari wani lokaci na gano ƙwayar cutar a farkon matakan cutar. A cikin matakai na gaba, ana buƙatar gwaji don gano ƙwayoyin cutar (IgM). Matakin magance cutar:kamuwa da zazzaɓin masassara na iya haifar da mummunar cuta kuma na iya zama mai kisa a lokuta masu tsanani. tallafin magani na farko a asibitoci yana inganta ƙimar rayuwa. A halin yanzu babu wani takamaiman maganin rigakafin ƙwayar cuta don zazzaɓi, amma takamaiman kulawa don magance rashin isashshen ruwa a jiki, zazzabi, da gazawar hanta da koda yana inganta sakamako. Ana iya magance ƙwayoyin cuta masu alaƙa da maganin rigakafi. Marasa lafiya suna buƙatar zama a gidan sauro da rana don irage haɗarin yadawa ga wasu ta hanyar cizon sauro. Alurar riga kafi ita ce hanya mafi mahimmanci don hana zazzabi. Za a iya hana barkewar cutar ne kawai idan an yiwa yawancin jama'a allurar rigakafi.
translations.reference: More severe cases can be confused with severe malaria, leptospirosis, viral hepatitis (especially fulminant forms), other haemorrhagic fevers, infection with other flaviviruses (such as dengue haemorrhagic fever) and poisoning. Polymerase chain reaction (PCR) testing in blood and urine can sometimes detect the virus in early stages of the disease. In later stages, testing to identify antibodies is needed (IgM). Treatment: Yellow fever infections can cause serious illness and can be fatal in severe cases. Early supportive treatment in hospitals improves survival rates. There is currently no specific anti-viral drug for yellow fever, but specific care to treat dehydration, fever, and liver and kidney failure improves outcomes. Associated bacterial infections can be treated with antibiotics. Patients need to stay under mosquito nets during the day to limit the risk of spreading to others through mosquito bites. Vaccination is the single most important measure for preventing yellow fever. The prevention of outbreaks can only be achieved if the majority of the population is immunized.

translations.source: Maganin cutar zazzabin shawara yana da aminci kuma mai araha, kuma kashi ɗaya na alluran rigakafi yana ba da rigakafi na tsawon rayuwa daga cutar. Mutanen da suka warke daga kamuwa da cutar zazzaɓi kuma suna da kariya har tsawon rayuwars. ƙara karantawa game da allurar rigakafin zazzaɓin cizon sauro. Kula da sauro na iya taimakawa wajen hana zazzabin rawaya kuma yana da mahimmanci a cikin yanayin da allurar rigakafi ba ta da yawa ko kuma ba a samun rigakafin nan da nan. Kula da sauro ya haɗa da kawar da wuraren da sauro ke hayayyafa da kashe manya sauro da tsutsa ta hanyar amfani da maganin kashe kwari a wuraren da ke da yawan sauro. Shigar da al'umma ta hanyar ayyuka, kamar tsaftace magudanar ruwa da kuma rufe kwantena na ruwa inda sauro ke iya hayayyafa hanya ce mai mahimmanci kuma mai inganci don magance sauro.
translations.reference: The yellow fever vaccine is safe and affordable, and a single dose provides life-long immunity against the disease. People who recover from yellow fever infection are also protected for life. Read more about the yellow fever vaccine. Mosquito control can also help to prevent yellow fever and is vital in situations where vaccination coverage is low or the vaccine is not immediately available. Mosquito control includes eliminating sites where mosquitoes can breed and killing adult mosquitoes and larvae by using insecticides in areas with high mosquito density. Community involvement through activities, such as cleaning household drains and covering water containers where mosquitoes can breed is an important and effective way to control mosquitoes.

translations.source: An bar mutanen da ke da buƙatar kulawar likita a baya cikin raɗaɗi, sabon rahoto ya bayyana. Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya (WHO) ta fitar da wani sabon rahoto kan samun damar yin amfani da sinadarin morphine a matsayin magani, wanda ya bayyana yadda rarraba morphine a duniya a matsayin maganin raɗaɗi mai mahimmanci, bai daidaita ba kuma baya biyan bukatun likita.B50. Rahoton, mai taken "cikin raɗaɗi", yana nuna matsalolin samun damar yin amfani da wannan magani mai mahimmanci tare da bayyana hanyoyin samar da shi cikin ta hanyar daidaitattun manufofi. Duk da cewa morphine magani ne mai inganci kuma mai sauƙin farashi don kawar da raɗaɗi mai ƙarfi, wanda aka jera tun 1977 a cikin wallafar WHO ta farko na jerin samfuran Mahimman Magunguna, rarrabuwar kai a cikin ƙasashe yana da ƙarfi. Akwai bambanci ninki 5 zuwa 63 a kiyasin matsakaicin yawan amfani da morphine tsakanin ƙasashe masu samun kuɗin shiga da ƙasashe masu ƙaramin karfi. Yanayin amfani ya bambanta sosai a cikin ƙasashe masu irin wannan arziki; Bai dace da buƙatun kiwon lafiya ba, kamar yadda ƙiyasin adadin kwanakin da mutane ke fama da raɗaɗi ko fama da matsanancin ƙarancin numfashi ga masu fama da rashin lafiyan da ba na tashi ba. Wadannan bayanan sun yi daidai da na Hukumar Lancet ta 2018 wacce ta bayyana rashin samun damar yin amfani da magungunan rage jin zafi a matsayin daya daga cikin rashin adalci mafi muni, a harkar kiwon lafiya ta duniya tare da 10% mafiya arzikin ƙasashe da ke daukar kashi 90% na morphine da aka rarraba. daidai opioids. Barin mutane cikin raɗaɗi a lokacin da ake samun ingantattun magunguna don magance raɗaɗin ciwo, musamman a yanayin kula da lafiya na karshen rayuwa, ya kamata ya zama abin damuwa ga masu tsara manufofi, in ji Dokta Yukiko Nakatani, Mataimakin Daraktan WHO. -Gabaɗaya don Magunguna da Kayayyakin Lafiya. Dole ne mu yi kira da gaggawa a samar da morphine kan lokaci ga waɗanda ke cikin buƙatun likita ta hanyar madaidaitan manufofi, a ko'ina.
translations.reference: People with medical needs are “left behind in pain” reveals new report The World Health Organization (WHO) has published a new report on access to morphine for medical use, which describes how the global distribution of morphine, as a vital pain medicine, is unequal and does not fulfil the medical need. The report, titled “Left behind in pain”, highlights the problems in access to this essential medicine and offers actions to improve safe access through balanced policy. Despite morphine being an effective and relatively low-cost medicine for relieving strong pain, listed since 1977 in the first edition of the WHO Model list of Essential Medicines, the disparity in access across countries is stark. There is a 5- to 63-fold difference in the estimated median consumption of morphine between high-income countries and lower income countries. Consumption pattern varies significantly across countries of similar wealth; it does not correspond to medical need, as indicated by the estimated number of days people are in pain or suffer with severe shortness of breath for people with a terminal illness. This data echoes that of the 2018 Lancet Commission which described the lack of access to pain relief medication as ‘one of the most heinous, hidden inequities in global health’ with the richest 10% of countries possessing 90% of distributed morphine-equivalent opioids. “Leaving people in pain when effective medicines are available for pain management, especially in the context of end-of-life care, should be a cause of serious concern for policy-makers,” says Dr Yukiko Nakatani, WHO Assistant Director-General for Medicines and Health Products. “We must urgently advocate for safe and timely access to morphine for those in medical need through balanced policy, everywhere.”

“A new report indicates that many people in need of medical care are being denied access. The World Health Organization (WHO) has released a new report on access to the opioid painkiller morphine, which details how morphine distribution around the world is crucial, yet uneven and does not meet medical needs. The report, titled “Within Pain,” highlights the challenges in accessing this essential medication, outlining methods for its production with standardized objectives.  Despite morphine being an effective and relatively inexpensive treatment for severe pain, it has been listed since 1977 in the first list of the WHO’s Essential Medicines list, distribution remains highly concentrated. There is a range of 5 to 63 percent difference in the average per capita morphine usage between high-income countries and low-income countries. Usage patterns vary significantly between countries of similar wealth; it does not align with healthcare needs, as the average number of days people spend with pain or severe shortness of breath in those with non-respiratory illnesses is disproportionate. These findings align with the 2018 Lancet report, which identified the lack of access to pain relief medications as one of the most unjust issues in global healthcare, with 90% of morphine and opioids being distributed by countries accounting for 10% of the world’s population. Leaving people in pain when effective medications are available to treat pain, particularly in palliative care settings, should be a concern for policymakers, according to Dr. Yukiko Nakatani, Deputy Director for Medicines and Health Products at the WHO. – Globally for Medicines and Health Products. We urgently need to accelerate the production of morphine for those in need of medical care, globally and equitably.”

**Notes on the translation:**
